question: What is the minimum distance between the handrails of escalators? Under NBC, the minimum width between balusters in an escalator.
keyMemory: Kapag escalator minimum width between balustrades/balusters ang tinanong under NBC reviewer logic, ang tandaan mo ay 558 mm.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 558 mm.
Ito, gusto kong maging maingat tayo sa wording. Medyo halo ang tanong kasi sinabi niya distance between the handrails, tapos sinundan ng minimum width between balusters in an escalator. Sa ALE reviewer logic, ang intended answer dito ay iyong NBC-based figure na minimum width between balustrades/balusters, at iyan ay 558 mm. Maraming reviewer references na lumabas sa search results ang consistent dito: "the width between balustrades escalator steps shall not be less than 558 mm," at may direct reviewer entries din na "Under NBC, the minimum width between balusters in an escalator — 558 mm." (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang importanteng clue ay iyong phrase na "Under NBC" plus minimum width. Iyan ay code-number memory item. Hindi ito estimate, hindi ito "parang practical." Exact value ang hanap. At sa reviewer/NBC-based references, ang minimum width between balustrades ay 558 mm. (Scribd)
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot
Kapag may choices na:
• 558
• 600
• 650
• 658
at ang tanong ay minimum under code, huwag kang matukso sa mukhang "rounded" or "safe-looking" number gaya ng 600. Board exam loves specific awkward numbers. Iyong mga hindi masyadong rounded, madalas iyan ang totoong code figure.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. 600 mm — mukhang tempting kasi clean number siya, pero hindi ito ang NBC-based figure na gusto ng reviewer.
c. 658 mm — mukhang ginawa lang para guluhin ka dahil kahawig ng 558.
d. 650 mm — same idea, plausible-sounding pero hindi exact code value.
Elimination method
Ganito mo siya i-eliminate sa actual exam:
Una, tanungin mo:
"Code figure ba ito o practical estimate?"
Code figure ito.
Pangalawa:
Kapag code figure, iwasan mo muna ang "rounded numbers" kung may isang mas specific na number.
Pangatlo:
Sa choices, ang pinaka-code-looking dito ay 558 mm.
At iyon nga ang consistent na lumalabas sa NBC/reviewer references. (Scribd)
Common trap sa board exam
Ang common trap dito ay 600 mm, kasi madaling tandaan at mukhang standard. Pero tandaan:
Madaling tandaan does not always mean tamang code figure.
Philippine basis
Sa local reviewer/NBC references na lumabas sa search results, the escalator width between balustrades is stated as not less than 558 mm. (Scribd)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag escalator minimum width between balustrades/balusters ang tinanong under NBC reviewer logic, ang tandaan mo ay 558 mm.
________________________________________

question: A continuous belt (chain) attached to the handrail and directly connected to the steps is known as ____.
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong ay continuous belt or chain loop sa escalator terminology, ang sagot ay Endless belt.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Endless belt.
Ito, terminology question ito. At maganda rito, may direct reviewer references na eksaktong parehong tanong at parehong sagot: Endless belt. (Quizlet)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang tanong mismo ay nagde-describe ng isang continuous belt/chain arrangement na tuloy-tuloy ang ikot at integrated sa handrail movement. Sa reviewer terminology, ang tawag dito ay endless belt. May search results na verbatim halos: "A continuous belt (chain) attached to the handrail and directly connected to the steps is known as _____. Endless belt." (Quizlet)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Handrail assembly — masyadong broad ito. Assembly ito ng handrail components, hindi specific doon sa continuous belt/chain na tinutukoy.
b. Balustrade assembly — ito naman iyong side structure/support ng escalator, hindi belt/chain connection.
d. Sprocket assembly — puwede itong bahagi ng drive mechanism, pero hindi ito ang term para sa mismong continuous belt described in the question.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot
Kapag ang tanong ay:
• nagde-describe ng moving loop
• tuloy-tuloy
• belt/chain form
• directly involved in motion
hanapin mo iyong term na may idea ng continuous loop.
At ang salitang pinakamalakas doon ay endless.
Elimination method
Step by step:
Una, alisin ang mga assembly terms na masyadong broad:
• handrail assembly
• balustrade assembly
Pangalawa, pag-isipan ang remaining choices:
• endless belt
• sprocket assembly
Ngayon, ano ang dine-describe ng tanong?
Hindi gear or toothed wheel.
Hindi sprocket.
Ang dine-describe ay belt/chain loop.
Kaya ang natitira ay Endless belt. (Wayground)
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay handrail assembly dahil nabanggit ang handrail sa tanong. Pero tandaan:
Hindi lahat ng term na inuulit ang word sa tanong ay siyang sagot.
Minsan ang word sa tanong ay clue lang sa mas specific na component.
Philippine basis
Sa reviewer references used in ALE-style utilities questions, this exact item is consistently keyed to Endless belt. (Quizlet)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong ay continuous belt or chain loop sa escalator terminology, ang sagot ay Endless belt.
Sige, tuloy tayo.

question: Ceiling material that best absorbs sound
keyMemory: Kapag may choice na acoustical at ang tanong ay tungkol sa sound absorption, iyon agad ang pinaka-malakas na sagot.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Acoustical board.
Ito, ang pinakamalaking clue ay "best absorbs sound" at "ceiling material." Sa apat na choices, ang acoustical board ang talagang ginawa para sa sound absorption sa ceilings. Hindi lang siya basta board; purpose-made siya para kontrolin ang reverberation at bawasan ang reflected sound. Sa reviewer keys na lumabas, ito rin ang consistent na sagot. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Gypsum board at cement board ay mas matitigas at mas reflective. Hindi sila ang "best absorber."
Fiberboard puwedeng may sound benefit sa ilang gamit, pero mas broad na materyal term iyan. Sa ALE-style terminology, kapag may acoustical board, iyon ang mas exact at mas purpose-specific na sagot. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
"Alin dito ang talagang acoustic product, hindi lang ordinary board?"
• Tanggalin mo ang gypsum board at cement board kasi mas construction board sila kaysa sound-absorbing ceiling finish.
• Between fiberboard at acoustical board, piliin mo ang mas purpose-specific.
• Kaya ang matitira: Acoustical board.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may choice na acoustical at ang tanong ay tungkol sa sound absorption, iyon agad ang pinaka-malakas na sagot.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: This acoustical material is manufactured from rock wool, glass fibers, wood fibers, hair felt, etc. generally installed on wood or metal framing system.
keyMemory: Rock wool + glass fibers + framing cavity installation = Acoustical blanket.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Acoustical blanket.
Ito, halos definition na mismo ng acoustical blankets ang nasa tanong. Yung phrase na rock wool, glass fibers, wood fibers, hair felt tapos installed on wood or metal framing system — iyan mismo ang classic description ng acoustical blanket or insulation blanket. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang acoustical blanket ay fibrous material na inilalagay sa framing cavities para sa sound control. So kung sinabi ng tanong na nasa wood or metal framing siya ini-install, malaking clue na iyon.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Acoustical tile — usually exposed finish tile ito, hindi iyong fibrous blanket sa framing cavity.
Acoustical plaster — applied finish ito, hindi blanket form.
Acoustical foam — sound-absorbing foam panel ito, pero hindi iyon ang exact material description na nasa tanong. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ganito ang board strategy:
• Kapag fibrous materials ang binanggit at framing installation ang clue, isipin mo agad blanket.
• Huwag mahulog sa tile o plaster, dahil mas finish materials ang dating nila.
• Hanapin mo ang may form na tumutugma sa description: blanket.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Rock wool + glass fibers + framing cavity installation = Acoustical blanket.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: Which material would exhibit the highest sound absorption coefficient (SAC) value?
keyMemory: Sa choices na hard finishes versus carpet, carpet ang highest SAC.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Carpet.
Ito, practical acoustics ito. Sa mga choices, ang carpet ang may pinakamataas na absorption dahil malambot, fibrous, at hindi reflective tulad ng hard surfaces. Reviewer keys for this exact item also point to carpet, and in acoustics, hard dense finishes like marble and plastic generally reflect more sound than soft porous surfaces. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Marble — napakatigas at reflective.
Wood — may konting mas magandang behavior kaysa marble sa ilang cases, pero hindi pa rin siya kasing absorbent ng carpet.
Plastic — generally reflective din ito kumpara sa carpet. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Soft, porous, fibrous = better absorber
• Hard, smooth, dense = more reflective
So:
• marble, wood, plastic → mas reflective
• carpet → pinaka-absorptive sa choices
Common trap
Huwag kang magpapadala sa "wood looks natural so maybe acoustic." Hindi lahat ng natural material ay mataas agad ang sound absorption.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa choices na hard finishes versus carpet, carpet ang highest SAC.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: Which of the following factors affect/s room acoustics?
keyMemory: Sa room acoustics, hindi iisang factor lang ang gumagalaw. Room cavity, seating, at furnishings — lahat may epekto.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay All of the above.
Ito, very classic all of the above item. Room acoustics is affected by the room cavity itself, the seating, and the furnishings, because all of these change how sound is reflected, absorbed, and distributed inside the room. The reviewer sources for this exact item key it to All of the above. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Hindi naman sila totally mali. Ang problema lang, bitin sila.
• Room cavity affects acoustics, yes.
• Seating affects acoustics, yes.
• Other furnishings also affect acoustics, yes.
Pero dahil lahat tama, ang pinaka-complete answer ay All of the above. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Kapag may all of the above, gawin mo ito:
1. Check mo kung may kahit isang maling item sa a, b, c.
2. Kapag lahat valid, piliin mo si d.
Common trap
Minsan ang examinee pumipili ng pinakakilala niyang factor, like seating, tapos hindi na tinitingnan ang kabuuan. Huwag ganoon. I-check lagi kung may mas complete choice.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa room acoustics, hindi iisang factor lang ang gumagalaw. Room cavity, seating, at furnishings — lahat may epekto.
________________________________________

question: When sound impinges on a surface such as walls, floor, ceiling, etc. it is referred to as ____.
keyMemory: Kapag ang tunog ay ina-analyze na in relation to walls, floors, ceilings, ALE reviewer logic points to structure borne sound.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Structure borne sound.
Dito, gusto kong i-highlight ang examiner's wording. Sa reviewer sources na tumutugma sa exact question, the keyed answer is structureborne sound. Ang logic nito: kapag ang sound ay tumama na sa wall, floor, o ceiling at naipasa na sa structural element, ang emphasis ng tanong ay nasa transmission through the building structure. (Scribd)
Bakit hindi airborne sound?
Kasi airborne sound is sound traveling through air. Pero ang tanong mismo ay may clue na impinges on a surface such as walls, floor, ceiling. Ibig sabihin, nasa interaction na siya with the building structure. Sa ALE-style reviewer logic, doon na pumapasok ang structureborne sound. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Liquidborne sound — malinaw na hindi ito, kasi hindi liquid ang medium sa tanong.
Solidborne sound — malapit pakinggan, pero ang term na ginagamit sa keyed reviewer item ay structureborne sound.
Airborne sound — ito sana kung nasa air-path pa lang ang emphasis ng tanong, pero hindi iyon ang wording dito. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ganito ang isipin mo:
• May wall/floor/ceiling na bang involved?
• Oo. So hindi na pure airborne ang focus.
• Hanapin mo ang term na mas tied sa building structure.
• Iyon ay structure borne sound.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay airborne sound, dahil sound naman usually travels in air. Pero kapag nakita mo ang clue na tumama na sa structure, mag-shift ka na sa structureborne.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tunog ay ina-analyze na in relation to walls, floors, ceilings, ALE reviewer logic points to structure borne sound.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: Acoustical cotton fiber composite board is manufactured using BAP. What does BAP stand for?
keyMemory: Sa item na ito, tandaan mo: BAP = Bonded Acoustical Panel.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Bonded acoustical panel.
Dito, may ilang reviewer copies online na inconsistent, pero the stronger keyed references for this exact item point to Bonded Acoustical Panel, and that also makes more technical sense in the context of cotton-fiber composite acoustic panels. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang word na bonded ang may tamang manufacturing logic para sa acoustical cotton fiber composite board — fibers are bonded into a panel form. Kaya sa choices, Bonded acoustical panel ang pinaka-plausible at pinaka-supported na answer. (Docsity)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Boarded acoustical panel — may ilang reviewer copies na ganyan ang lumalabas, pero mas mukhang typo or weak copy variant.
Beaded and pressed — hindi ito standard expansion ng BAP dito.
Bent and polished — obviously hindi ito manufacturing term para sa acoustical cotton board. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ganito ang pag-iisip:
• Composite board ito, so malamang ang process ay bonding.
• Tanggal agad ang choices na parang generic action words lang: beaded and pressed, bent and polished.
• Between boarded and bonded, mas technical at mas logical ang bonded.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay boarded acoustical panel kasi kahawig ng "board" sa tanong. Pero huwag kang padadala sa tunog lang. Mas mahalaga ang manufacturing logic at mas reliable keyed references.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa item na ito, tandaan mo: BAP = Bonded Acoustical Panel.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: It is the product of surface area (sq.ft.) and sound absorption coefficient (SAC). It has the unit sabin.
keyMemory: Area × SAC = Sound absorption, at ang unit niya ay sabin.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Sound absorption.
Ito, very important acoustics concept ito. The product of surface area × sound absorption coefficient is sound absorption, and its unit is sabins. That is both a reviewer key and a standard acoustics definition. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kapag area times SAC ang kinukwenta mo, ang lumalabas ay total sound absorption in sabins. Kaya sound absorption talaga ang sagot, hindi attenuation, amplification, o noise reduction. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Sound attenuation — broader reduction concept ito habang nagta-travel ang sound, hindi specific formula na area × SAC.
Sound amplification — kabaligtaran pa nga ito ng absorption.
Noise reduction — result or objective ito, pero hindi iyon ang exact quantity defined by area × SAC. (acoustic-glossary.co.uk)
Elimination method
Kapag nakita mo ang clues:
• surface area
• SAC
• unit sabin
automatic mo nang isipin: sound absorption.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay noise reduction, kasi parang iyon naman ang dulo ng acoustic treatment. Pero ang tanong ay hindi objective. Ang tanong ay specific measured quantity.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Area × SAC = Sound absorption, at ang unit niya ay sabin.
________________________________________

question: Wallace Clement Sabine, an American physicist who pioneered work on sound is said to be the father of ____.
keyMemory: Wallace Clement Sabine = father of architectural acoustics.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Architectural acoustics.
Ito, napaka-famous nito. Wallace Clement Sabine is widely recognized as the father or founder of architectural acoustics. That is how Britannica and other acoustics history sources describe him, and reviewer materials use the same answer. (Encyclopedia Britannica)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Si Sabine ang nagbigay ng scientific basis sa room reverberation and acoustic design of spaces. Kaya ang field na pinaka-direct na ikinakabit sa kanya ay architectural acoustics. (Encyclopedia Britannica)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Environmental acoustics — ibang branch ito, mas tungkol sa environmental noise.
Room acoustics — related, pero mas makitid at hindi iyan ang pinakasikat na title na ibinibigay kay Sabine.
Acoustical science — masyadong broad at hindi iyon ang standard phrase. (Studocu)
Elimination method
Kapag may tanong tungkol kay Sabine:
• isipin mo agad ang design of buildings and rooms for good sound
• hindi environment
• hindi generic science
• kaya architectural acoustics
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Wallace Clement Sabine = father of architectural acoustics.
________________________________________

answerConceptVisual.title: Key concept / value
answerConceptVisual.detail: Memorize the exact ALE/reviewer value or term.

question: A condition characterized as vertically downward directly below the luminaire. What is that term?
keyMemory: Sa lighting terms, directly below the luminaire = Nadir.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Nadir.
Ito, lighting terminology ito. The reviewer sources for this exact wording key it to Nadir, which means the point or direction vertically downward directly below the luminaire or observer. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang clue ay "vertically downward directly below the luminaire."
Iyan mismo ang idea ng nadir — downward vertical direction.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Lumen — unit ito ng luminous flux, hindi direction.
Point source — uri ito ng light source assumption, hindi direction below a luminaire.
Lambert — unit of luminance/brightness, hindi rin direction. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ganito lang:
• Tanong ba ay unit? Hindi.
• Tanong ba ay type of source? Hindi.
• Tanong ay direction/position directly below.
• So ang natitira ay Nadir.
Common trap
Minsan pinipili ang lumen dahil familiar word siya sa lighting. Pero tandaan:
lumen = quantity of light, not direction.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa lighting terms, directly below the luminaire = Nadir.
Sige, tuloy tayo.

question: What is PABX?
keyMemory: PABX = Private Automatic Branch Exchange.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay private automatic branch exchange.
Ito, acronym identification lang siya, pero classic reviewer trap ito dahil tatlo sa choices mukhang technical pakinggan pero gawa-gawa lang ang dating.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa telephony context, ang PABX/PBX ay private branch exchange system used within an enterprise or private organization. Cisco uses the phrase private automatic branch exchange in its voice documentation, so sa choices, si private automatic branch exchange ang tumatama. (Cisco)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. public announcement box exchange — halatang maling construction ng term.
c. public automatic box exchange — may "automatic" at "exchange," pero mali ang "public" at "box."
d. public announcement branch exchange — pinagsama lang ang words para magmukhang technical.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
• Ang P sa PABX, sa telecom, halos siguradong private, hindi public.
• Ang BX usually points to branch exchange.
• So tanggal agad ang may public.
• Then sa natitira, piliin ang mukhang tunay na telecom term.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay ‘yung choices na maraming technical-sounding words.
Pero tandaan: sa abbreviations, kadalasan isang choice lang ang existing industry term.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
PABX = Private Automatic Branch Exchange.

question: What is BMS for a project?
keyMemory: Sa smart buildings at utilities coordination, BMS = Building Management System.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay building management system.
Ito, very important ito sa smart buildings at building utilities integration.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang BMS stands for Building Management System. IBM describes BMS as control systems that centralize building management functions like lighting, air-conditioning, maintenance, and safety into one platform. Iyan mismo ang gamit sa building projects. (IBM)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. broadcast microwave services — communications term ang tunog, hindi building utilities control platform.
c. bachelor of management services — obviously hindi project/system term.
d. building management services — malapit pakinggan, pero ang accepted standard expansion ng BMS ay system, hindi services. (IBM)
Elimination method
Ganito ang bilis ng isip mo dapat:
• Project/building context ba? Oo.
• Control/integration platform ba ang hinahanap? Oo.
• Hanapin ang may salitang system, hindi services.
Common trap sa board exam
Madalas dito nadadale ang examinee sa building management services dahil mukhang mas natural pakinggan.
Pero sa actual industry acronym, BMS = system.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa smart buildings at utilities coordination, BMS = Building Management System.

question: What is a disadvantage of WLAN?

choices.text: no security on the level of fixed networks
devices will only operate at a limited distance from an access point

answer: C. devices will only operate at a limited distance from an access point
keyMemory: Sa WLAN, ang pinaka-basic disadvantage na madaling i-defend sa exam ay limited range from the access point.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay devices will only operate at a limited distance from an access point.
Ito, magandang reasoning item ito. Hindi lang acronym memory.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang tunay na basic disadvantage ng WLAN ay range or coverage limitation. Cisco's WLAN design guidance emphasizes that wireless coverage depends on AP placement, coverage planning, and client density, which means devices are effectively limited by distance and coverage from the access point. (Cisco)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. limited access to central applications — hindi ito inherent disadvantage ng WLAN. In fact, Cisco notes the goal of WLAN LAN extension is to provide the same applications and services as a wired access network. (Cisco)
b. no security on the level of fixed networks — mali ang absolute wording. Cisco even notes that with proper implementation, WLAN security can be equivalent to, or in some cases stronger than, many wired networks. (Cisco)
d. limited voice and data services — hindi rin ito tama as a general statement; Cisco documents that wireless networks can be deployed for data, voice, video, and location-aware services. (Cisco)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Hanapin ang real physical limitation ng wireless.
• Ang pinaka-basic at pinaka-obvious ay coverage/range.
• Tanggalin ang choices na masyadong absolute tulad ng no security.
• Tanggalin ang choices na hindi inherent limitation tulad ng limited access to central apps at limited voice/data services.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay b, kasi maraming tao ang automatic iniisip na insecure ang wireless.
Pero board strategy: iwasan ang choice na may extreme wording na "no security". Sobrang absolute niyan, kaya malamang mali.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa WLAN, ang pinaka-basic disadvantage na madaling i-defend sa exam ay limited range from the access point.
________________________________________

question: Maximum height of dumbwaiter?
keyMemory: Maximum dumbwaiter height under NBC = 1.2 meters.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 1.2 meters.
Ito, code-based na ito. At dito papasok ang Philippine basis.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa 2004 Revised IRR of the National Building Code of the Philippines, ang dumbwaiter is defined as a hoisting mechanism for small materials, with a car or shelved enclosure having a maximum height of 1.20 meters. So exact NBC figure ito, hindi estimate lang. (PRB Architecture)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
2.1 m, 1.8 m, at 2.4 m — lahat iyan lampas sa code definition limit ng dumbwaiter car/enclosure height under NBC. (PRB Architecture)
Elimination method
Ganito ang code-question mindset:
• Kapag ang tanong ay maximum height at code-based, huwag mamili ng "mukhang practical."
• Hanapin ang exact memorized figure.
• Sa dumbwaiter, maliit lang talaga ito dahil hindi siya passenger elevator.
• Kaya ang pinaka-logical at pinaka-code-correct ay 1.2 m.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay pipili ng 1.8 m o 2.1 m dahil iniisip na para mas kasya ang items.
Pero tandaan: dumbwaiter is for small materials, so intentionally limited ang size niya.
Philippine basis
Direkta ito sa NBCP Revised IRR definition ng dumbwaiter. (PRB Architecture)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Maximum dumbwaiter height under NBC = 1.2 meters.
________________________________________

answerConceptVisual.detail: Memorize the exact ALE/reviewer value or term.

question: Type of sprinkler used for "open ceiling" designed rooms?
keyMemory: Kapag open ceiling / exposed piping ang condition, ang best answer ay Upright sprinkler.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Upright.
Ito, magandang fire protection identification item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang upright sprinkler ang karaniwang ginagamit kung open ceiling / exposed piping ang setup. NFPA describes upright sprinklers as mounted on top of branch lines or sprigs, and sprinkler product literature also distinguishes upright sprinklers for exposed pipe systems, while finished ceilings commonly use pendent or recessed arrangements. Ibig sabihin, kapag open ceiling design ang room, upright ang pinaka-malamang na sagot. (NFPA)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Pendent — mas common ito sa finished ceilings or suspended ceiling conditions, lalo na kung nakabitin pababa from concealed or below-ceiling piping. (reliablesprinkler.com)
b. side throw — ginagamit ito kung sidewall or special directional discharge condition ang kailangan, hindi ito ang general answer para sa open ceiling rooms.
d. dry type — ginagamit ito para sa areas exposed to freezing or where the sprinkler barrel must stay dry until activation, hindi dahil lang open ceiling ang design. (Viking Group Inc.)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Open ceiling = exposed pipes, visible services
• Kapag exposed pipe system, mas natural ang upright
• Pendent isipin mo kapag typical finished ceiling application
• Dry type isipin mo kapag cold/freezing concern
• Side throw isipin mo kapag sidewall/directional condition
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay pendent, kasi iyon ang pinakakilalang sprinkler ng maraming examinee.
Pero basahin ang keyword: open ceiling.
Hindi finished ceiling.
Kaya dapat mag-shift ka sa upright.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag open ceiling / exposed piping ang condition, ang best answer ay Upright sprinkler.
Sige, tuloy tayo.
answerImageCredit: Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons first; Google Images fallback only.

question: For a convenience store with a floor area of 100 sqm, with full window glass on its front and sides, what is the ideal air conditioning system to be used?
keyMemory: Kapag retail/open commercial space ang tanong, ang safe ALE answer ay madalas cassette-type split, hindi window type.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Cassette type – Split type.
Ito, ang clue dito ay 100 sqm, convenience store, at full window glass sa front and sides. Ibig sabihin, open commercial space ito na may malaking heat gain. Sa mga choices, ang pinaka-bagay ay cassette type split dahil ang cassette units are commonly used for retail shops and commercial open spaces, habang ang central AC is usually for a whole-house or ducted whole-space setup, at ang window type is really for smaller isolated rooms. (Daikin Internet)
Bakit hindi iyong iba? Window type masyadong bitin para sa ganitong open commercial setup. Centralized puwede sa mas malaking building system, pero para sa isang 100-sqm convenience store, kadalasan overkill at hindi ito ang pinaka-practical na ALE answer. Floor mounted split may gamit iyan kapag may layout constraints, pero sa retail/open plan, mas maganda ang overhead, mas even ang distribution, at mas hindi sagabal sa shelves at circulation. Dito, cassette ang natitira dahil practical, commercial, at bagay sa open sales area. (Daikin Internet)
Elimination method:
Kapag nakita mo ang commercial open space, alisin mo agad ang window type. Kapag isang medium commercial space lang at hindi full-building ducted system, huwag agad centralized. Kapag gusto mo ng mas malinis na air distribution at hindi sagabal sa merchandise layout, cassette split ang pinakamalakas.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag retail/open commercial space ang tanong, ang safe ALE answer ay madalas cassette-type split, hindi window type.

question: A safety device for preventing the operation of an elevator car unless its door or gate is fully closed.
keyMemory: Sa ganitong reviewer wording, Door contact ang sagot kapag ang elevator ay hindi dapat umandar hangga't hindi fully closed ang pinto o gate.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Door contact.
Dito, gusto kong maging maingat. Sa real elevator terminology, may interlock/lock concepts talaga. Pero sa choices na ibinigay, ang pinakamalapit at pinaka-intended answer ay door contact. May code descriptions and safety references na nagsasabing the elevator should not operate unless the car door or gate is at or near full closure, and a car door contact is specifically described as the feature that prevents the elevator from running unless the car gate is closed. (Pennsylvania Code & Bulletin)
Bakit hindi gate lock? Kasi ang wording ng tanong ay nakatutok sa preventing operation unless fully closed, which is more the function of the electrical contact/safety contact in the exam wording here. Cable lock obviously ibang topic. Alarm hindi naman iyon ang nagpi-prevent ng operation; warning lang iyon. Kaya kahit alam mo na may "interlock" sa totoong elevator systems, dahil wala iyon sa choices, door contact ang best answer.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa hindi aandar hangga't hindi sarado ang pinto, hanapin mo ang choice na may kinalaman sa door-closure sensing. Huwag kang magpapadala sa "lock" kung ang tanong ay tungkol sa operating circuit condition.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ganitong reviewer wording, Door contact ang sagot kapag ang elevator ay hindi dapat umandar hangga't hindi fully closed ang pinto o gate.

question: Usually used for kitchen and toilets to expel odors?
keyMemory: Kapag toilets at kitchen odors ang tanong, automatic mong isipin: Exhaust fan.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Exhaust Fan.
Ito straightforward. Kitchens and toilets need a fan that removes odors, moisture, smoke, and stale air palabas ng space. Iyon ang eksaktong trabaho ng exhaust fan. Home ventilation guides describe exhaust fans as commonly used in kitchens and bathrooms to vent out odors, moisture, and contaminants. (Hanabishi)
Bakit mali ang iba? Ceiling fan umiikot lang ang hangin sa loob; hindi siya talaga naglalabas ng amoy. Transfer fan ginagamit to move air from one space to another, not primarily to expel odors outside. Mechanical fan sobrang broad; generic category lang iyan. So kapag specific ang function na expel odors, exhaust fan agad.
Elimination method:
Tandaan mo ito:
Move air within room = ceiling fan.
Move air between spaces = transfer fan.
Expel air out = exhaust fan.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag toilets at kitchen odors ang tanong, automatic mong isipin: Exhaust fan.

question: Where is the ideal location of the condensing unit of a window type air conditioning system?

choices.text: The nearest possible vacant space where the window type air conditioning system is located.
Window type air conditioning system does not have condensing unit.

answer: C. Window type air conditioning system does not have condensing unit.
keyMemory: Window type AC = walang hiwalay na condensing unit.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Window type air conditioning system does not have condensing unit.
Ito ang trap dito: baka mapaisip ka agad ng split-type logic. Pero window-type AC is a single self-contained unit. Nasa iisang casing na ang major components niya, kabilang ang condenser side and evaporator side. So wala siyang separate condensing unit na ilalagay pa somewhere else. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba? Roof deck at condensing room ay split o central-system style thinking iyan. Nearest possible vacant space ganyan din. Ang tanong specifically says window type, so ang tamang unang tanong sa isip mo dapat: "May hiwalay ba itong outdoor unit?" Sagot: wala.
Elimination method:
Kapag AC question, i-classify mo muna ang system:
Window type = monoblock/self-contained
Split type = may indoor at outdoor
Kapag mali ang system classification mo, mali na agad ang sagot mo.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Window type AC = walang hiwalay na condensing unit.

question: Maximum cars for an elevator bank?
keyMemory: Sa ALE reviewer logic, maximum cars for an elevator bank = 4.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 4.
Ito, gusto kong maging honest: ito ay more on reviewer/planning convention kaysa direct Philippine code line item sa tanong mismo. Sa ALE reviewer sources and elevator-planning flashcards, the keyed answer is 4, and common planning guidance also uses the rule that a bank should not have more than 4 cars in a row for efficiency and passenger handling. (Wayground)
Bakit hindi 6? Kasi kapag masyado nang marami sa iisang tuloy-tuloy na bank, bumababa ang efficiency at mas nagiging masalimuot ang passenger handling; in practice, larger groups are often split into separate banks or zones. Kaya sa board-review logic, 4 ang standard remembered answer.
Elimination method:
Kapag ganitong transportation planning item, hanapin mo ang commonly memorized planning rule, hindi yung "pinakamarami."
2 at 3 puwede pa rin, pero ang tanong ay maximum.
6 looks tempting, pero reviewer convention points to 4.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ALE reviewer logic, maximum cars for an elevator bank = 4.

question: It is a shaft for the travel of elevator or dumbwaiters.
keyMemory: Elevator or dumbwaiter shaft = Hoistway.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Hoistway.
Ang hoistway ang vertical shaft or space where the elevator travels. TK Elevator even defines the hoistway as the shaft, shaftway, or hatchway in which the elevator runs. Kaya ito ang exact term. (TK Elevator)
Bakit mali ang iba? Stack at riser are plumbing or services vertical lines. Guide way sounds close, pero hindi iyan ang standard shaft term sa elevator planning. The guide rails are inside the hoistway, pero ang buong shaft itself is the hoistway.
Elimination method:
Kapag elevator shaft ang usapan, huwag mong ihalo sa plumbing vertical terms.
Riser/stack = utilities.
Hoistway = elevator shaft.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Elevator or dumbwaiter shaft = Hoistway.

question: It is a process supplying or removing air by natural or mechanical means.
keyMemory: Supplying/removing air = Ventilation.
explanation:

Ito ay textbook definition. Ventilation is the process of supplying and removing air from a room or building by natural or mechanical means. Iyan mismo ang wording ng ventilation references. (AIVC)
Bakit mali ang iba? Air conditioning mas malawak at may temperature/humidity control pa. HVAC umbrella term iyon. Cooling naman one function lang. Ang tanong ay sobrang specific sa supplying or removing air, kaya ventilation talaga.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol lang sa air exchange, huwag mong piliin ang mas malawak na system term gaya ng HVAC o air conditioning.

Supplying/removing air = Ventilation.

question: The frame on both side of escalator?
keyMemory: Escalator side frame assembly = Balustrade.
explanation:

Sa escalator terminology, the balustrade is the side assembly or side structure that extends above the steps and supports the handrail. Elevator industry glossaries define the balustrade as the side of the escalator and the assembly that includes panels and handrail support. (TK Elevator)
Bakit hindi railing? Kasi mas casual word iyon. Sa board exam, ang gusto nila ay exact technical term. Side buffer at guide ay hindi iyan ang standard name ng side frame assembly.
Elimination method:
Kapag nakita mo ang escalator-side framing + handrail support, huwag mong piliin ang ordinary word na railing kung may mas exact term na balustrade.

Escalator side frame assembly = Balustrade.

question: Which of the following has a plunger?
keyMemory: Kapag plunger ang clue, ang sagot ay Hydraulic Elevator.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Hydraulic Elevator.
Hydraulic elevators use a cylinder and plunger arrangement to raise the car. Britannica specifically notes that hydraulic cylinders and plungers are used for hydraulic elevators. Kaya kapag nakita mo ang salitang plunger, halos hydraulic agad iyan. (Encyclopedia Britannica)
Bakit mali ang iba? Geared, gearless, at electric/traction elevator systems are rope-and-sheave or motor-driven systems, not plunger-based lifting systems.
Elimination method:
Keyword spotting ito:
Plunger = hydraulic
Gear/sheave/ropes = traction/electric
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag plunger ang clue, ang sagot ay Hydraulic Elevator.

question: Which of the following has a gear and worm?
keyMemory: Worm gear = Geared Elevator.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Geared Elevator.
Ito diretso. Geared traction machines use a worm-and-gear reduction unit to turn the hoisting sheave. That is exactly why they are called geared elevators. (Wikipedia)
Bakit mali ang iba? Gear-less by definition walang gearbox reduction. Hydraulic uses fluid pressure. Electric elevator is too broad and not the exact type being asked here.
Elimination method:
Kapag may clue na gear and worm, huwag ka nang lumayo. Hanapin mo iyong may exact word match and actual mechanism match: Geared Elevator.

question: Which of the following is the best for high rise?
keyMemory: High-rise = Gearless traction elevator.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Gear-less Elevator.
For high-rise buildings, the best choice among those options is gearless. Elevator manufacturers and industry references describe gearless traction elevators as the clear choice or the most common solution for high-rises because of their higher speed and lifting capacity. (TK Elevator)
Bakit mali ang iba? Hydraulic is for low-rise. Geared is more for low-to-mid-rise. Electric elevator is too broad; puwede niyang sakupin geared or gearless, so hindi siya ang exact answer. Sa board exam, kapag may broad at may exact, piliin mo ang exact.
Elimination method:
High-rise question ito. So tanggal agad hydraulic. Then between geared and gearless, piliin ang mas high-performance system for tall buildings: gearless.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
High-rise = Gearless traction elevator.

question: Which of the following is NOT ideal for high rise building?
keyMemory: Sa elevator context, Hydraulic Elevator ang hindi ideal sa high-rise.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Hydraulic Elevator.
Dito, medyo halo ang choices, pero dahil naka-cluster ang previous questions sa elevator types, ang intended answer ng examiner ay Hydraulic Elevator. Hydraulic elevators are commonly used for low-rise applications, while high-rise buildings typically use traction systems, especially gearless traction. (Encyclopedia Britannica)
Bakit hindi iyong iba? Electric elevator is the normal family for taller buildings. Escalator can still exist in high-rise buildings as a supplemental transport system on lower public floors. Dumbwaiter can also exist if needed for service. Split type air-conditioning may be less preferred in many high-rise projects, yes, pero in the context of this elevator sequence, the most clearly "not ideal for high rise" is hydraulic elevator. Ito ang ALE-style intended answer.
Elimination method:
Kapag magkakasunod ang tanong tungkol sa elevator mechanisms, sundan mo ang pattern.
Previous item: best for high-rise = gearless
So the opposite not-ideal type = hydraulic
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa elevator context, Hydraulic Elevator ang hindi ideal sa high-rise.

question: Elevators that are lifted by ropes, which pass over a wheel attached to an electric motor above the elevator shaft.
keyMemory: Rope over wheel/sheave is the signature clue of a Traction Elevator.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Traction Elevator.
Ito ay halos direct definition ng traction elevator. Traction elevators use ropes or belts that pass over a wheel or sheave connected to an electric motor to move the car. Elevator references describe exactly that mechanism. (CBA Consultants)
Bakit mali ang iba? Pneumatic uses pressure differences/vacuum concepts. Climbing elevator is self-ascending on a mast/tower. Hydraulic uses fluid pressure and piston/plunger. So kapag rope over sheave ang clue, traction agad.
Elimination method:
Ito ang quick memory mo:
Ropes + sheave + motor = traction
Oil + piston = hydraulic
Vacuum = pneumatic
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Rope over wheel/sheave is the signature clue of a Traction Elevator.

question: It is a self-ascending elevator with its own propulsion. The propulsion can be done by an electric or a combustion engine and used in guyed masts or towers, in order to make easy access to parts of these constructions, such as flight safety lamps for maintenance.
keyMemory: Tower/mast maintenance elevator = Climbing Elevator.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Climbing Elevator.
Ito, halos verbatim definition na ng climbing elevator. References describe a climbing elevator as a self-ascending elevator with its own propulsion, used on guyed masts or towers to access things like flight safety lamps for maintenance. (Wikipedia)
Bakit mali ang iba? Pneumatic not for towers in that way. Hydraulic hindi self-climbing mast elevator. Traction is rope/sheave inside shaft, not tower-climbing maintenance access equipment.
Elimination method:
Kapag may clue na:
• self-ascending
• own propulsion
• mast or tower
• maintenance access
iisa lang talaga ang babagay: Climbing Elevator.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Tower/mast maintenance elevator = Climbing Elevator.

question: Which is NOT an ideal location of ACCU in relation to the FCU?
keyMemory: Sa split-type systems, mas maikli ang refrigerant line, mas ideal. Kaya sa awkward item na ito, ang pinakamagandang sagot sa NOT ideal ay iyong 15-meter distance choice.
explanation:
Ulit: sa choices na ito, ang best answer ay With 15 meters distance.
Dito gusto kong maging honest: medyo awkward ang wording ng item, at mukhang may overlap ang choices within 15 meters at with 15 meters distance. So ang safest ALE reading dito ay: the longer run is not the ideal one. Many split-system installation manuals state that the shorter the refrigerant piping, the better the performance, while lengths around 15 m are often maximum allowable or near-maximum allowable values. They also show that an outdoor unit below the indoor unit can still be installed, subject to correct piping/drain detailing. (Daikin Internet)
So bakit d? Kasi kung ang tanong ay NOT ideal, hindi mo pipiliin iyong pinakamalapit or shortest arrangement. Within 3 meters is obviously ideal. 3 meter below the FCU can still be a valid installed condition. Within 15 meters still reads as allowable range. But with 15 meters distance sounds like you are already at the long end, which is usually allowable but not the ideal target, since manuals explicitly say shorter piping performs better. (Daikin Internet)
Elimination method:
Kapag split-type ACCU/FCU relation ang usapan, tandaan mo:
shorter piping = better
maximum allowable ≠ ideal
Kaya kapag may option na mukhang nasa pinakahabang run, iyon ang madalas na "NOT ideal."
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa split-type systems, mas maikli ang refrigerant line, mas ideal. Kaya sa awkward item na ito, ang pinakamagandang sagot sa NOT ideal ay iyong 15-meter distance choice.
Sige, tuloy tayo.

question: Ideal Air-Conditioning system for a walk-up apartment unit with an open plan Kitchen-Living-Dining Design covering 100 sqm total floor area. The unit has a balcony?
keyMemory: Kapag isang residential unit ang tanong at may puwesto para sa outdoor unit, split type ang madalas na best ALE answer.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Split type.
Dito, ang iniisip mo dapat ay anong system ang pinaka-practical at pinaka-angkop sa isang residential apartment unit, hindi sa buong building. Ang split system ay isang air-conditioning system na may indoor at outdoor unit na magka-connect lang by tubing, at ginagamit talaga ito sa residential settings. Samantalang ang centralized system at AHU-based systems ay pang mas malalaking multiroom or whole-building ducted setups. Kaya sa isang walk-up apartment unit, lalo na may balcony na puwedeng paglagyan ng outdoor unit, ang pinaka-board-logical na sagot ay split type. (Carrier)
Bakit mali ang iba? Window type mas pang maliit o isolated room, at hindi iyon ang pinaka-ideal sa ganitong apartment setup. Centralized at with AHU mas pang central HVAC arrangement iyan. Ducted type puwede sa ibang projects, pero para sa isang walk-up apartment, mas komplikado at mas hindi iyon ang pinaka-direct reviewer answer kung may simpler residential option sa choices. Kaya sa elimination, tanggalin mo muna ang pang-building systems, tapos alisin ang underpowered or less-ideal room-type unit, at ang matitira ay Split type. (Panasonic)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag isang residential unit ang tanong at may puwesto para sa outdoor unit, split type ang madalas na best ALE answer.

question: A consideration to provide at least one passenger elevator that will operate 24 hour constant service for apartments or residential condominiums?

choices.text: If Rent is 17 thousand a month per unit
If there is no fire exit
If the building is five storeys or more
If there is only one stairway and it is 6 storeys or more
If the occupants are generally 35 year old and older

answer: C. If the building is five storeys or more
keyMemory: Sa NBC reviewer logic: 5 storeys or more = at least one passenger elevator on 24-hour constant service.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay If the building is five storeys or more.
Ito, diretsong code item ito. Sa 2004 Revised IRR of the National Building Code of the Philippines, nakalagay na sa apartments or residential condominiums na five storeys or more, at least one passenger elevator shall be kept on 24-hour constant service. So hindi ito opinion question. Exact code trigger ito. (Arch Regulatory Board)
Bakit mali ang iba? Walang code basis ang monthly rent, age of occupants, o "no fire exit" bilang trigger para sa rule na ito. Yung one stairway and 6 storeys or more ay mukhang ginawa lang para malito ka dahil may number din, pero hindi iyon ang actual wording ng code requirement dito. Sa elimination method, kapag may isang choice na mukhang exact code threshold, iyon ang kumakapit. Dito, iyon ang five storeys or more. (Arch Regulatory Board)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa NBC reviewer logic: 5 storeys or more = at least one passenger elevator on 24-hour constant service.

question: What is the effective temperature of the air to be used for comfortable cooling?
keyMemory: Sa ALE/reviewer logic, comfortable cooling effective temperature = 20°C to 24°C.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 20°C to 24°C.
Dito, ALE reviewer style ang labanan. Sa reviewer and PSME-style references that match this item, the keyed answer for the effective temperature of the air to be used for comfortable cooling is 20°C to 24°C. So kahit may ibang modern comfort guides na nagbibigay ng slightly different room-temperature ranges depending on humidity, occupancy, and climate, ang board-review intended answer dito ay 20–24°C. (Ace Your Board Exam with PinoyBIX)
Bakit mali ang iba? 18–26°C at 20–26°C masyadong malawak; parang hindi na exact reviewer comfort band. 22–24°C naman masyadong makitid. Ito ang common trap: pipili ka ng range na mukhang "practical," pero ang tanong ay naghahanap ng reviewer-standard figure, hindi estimate. Kaya kapag gusto mong mag-eliminate, hanapin mo ang pinaka-kilala at pinaka-exact na comfort-cooling range sa reviewer references. Dito, 20–24°C ang natitira. (Ace Your Board Exam with PinoyBIX)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ALE/reviewer logic, comfortable cooling effective temperature = 20°C to 24°C.

question: It is a type of heating, ventilating, and/or air-conditioning (HVAC) system. With variable airflow at a constant temperature.
keyMemory: Variable airflow + constant temperature = VAV.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay VAV.
Ito napaka-classic. Variable Air Volume systems vary the airflow while keeping the supply-air temperature essentially constant. Iyan mismo ang defining clue ng tanong: variable airflow at constant temperature. (PNNL)
Bakit mali ang iba? CAV is the opposite idea — constant air volume. VRF/VRV are refrigerant-based multi-zone systems, not air-volume system terminology. Multi-split is a system arrangement of indoor and outdoor units, not the airflow-control concept being described. Sa elimination method, ang keyword mo rito ay airflow. Kapag airflow ang binabago, think VAV. Kapag refrigerant flow ang binabago, doon ka sa VRF/VRV. (PNNL)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Variable airflow + constant temperature = VAV.

question: It is a system which connects up to five indoor units to a single outdoor unit. Installs a complete air conditioning system to multiple zone interior spaces with no need for ductwork.
keyMemory: One outdoor unit + several indoor units, no ductwork = Multi-Split.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Multi-Split.
Ito halos verbatim na sa product descriptions ng manufacturers. Daikin and others describe multi-split as a system that can connect up to five indoor units to one outdoor unit, and do so without ductwork. Kaya sa choices, wala nang mas tatama pa kaysa Multi-Split. (Daikin)
Bakit mali ang iba? VAV at CAV are air distribution/control concepts. VRF/VRV are broader variable-refrigerant systems and can serve many indoor units, but the phrase "up to five indoor units to a single outdoor unit" is the more textbook/product-like description of multi-split in residential/light commercial use. Sa elimination method, kapag may exact numerical clue like up to five indoor units, huwag ka nang lumayo sa most direct system label. (Daikin)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
One outdoor unit + several indoor units, no ductwork = Multi-Split.

Ito definition question. Ang tamang generic technical term para sa fluid or working substance used in a refrigeration or heat-pump cycle is refrigerant. Hindi "Freon" ang general scientific term; brand/trade-associated term iyon for some specific refrigerants. (ScienceDirect)

question: A movable plate for regulating the draft in an air outlet, air ducts or the throat of a fireplace?
keyMemory: Movable airflow-regulating plate = Damper.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Damper.
A damper is literally a movable plate or valve used to regulate airflow in ducts, chimneys, and similar air passages. The word draft in the question is a huge clue. Sa HVAC at fireplace terminology, damper ang pang-regulate ng draft or airflow. (lennox.com)
Bakit mali ang iba? Louver usually refers to fixed or adjustable slats in an opening, not the classic draft-regulating plate in a duct or fireplace throat. Exhaust is a function, not the regulating plate itself. Ventilator is a device/system for air exchange. Diffuser spreads supply air into a room. Sa elimination method, tanungin mo: "Alin dito ang mismong plate na nagko-control ng airflow?" Iyon lang si Damper. (lennox.com)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Movable airflow-regulating plate = Damper.

question: A device used for banks to avoid power interruption?
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay avoid power interruption, sagot ay UPS, hindi AVR.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay UPS.
A UPS, or uninterruptible power supply, is specifically used to provide near-instant or short-term backup power when incoming utility power is interrupted. Iyan ang exact device for avoiding power interruption sa sensitive equipment and operations. (TechTarget)
Bakit mali ang iba? AVR regulates voltage but does not necessarily sustain power during an outage. ATS transfers source, usually to a generator or alternate source, but it is not itself the device that bridges power with no interruption. Router obviously communications device iyon. Sa elimination, tandaan mo:
AVR = stabilize voltage
ATS = transfer source
UPS = keep power alive through interruption. (TechTarget)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay avoid power interruption, sagot ay UPS, hindi AVR.

question: Which of the following lamp has the BEST CRI?
keyMemory: Kapag CRI ang laban, pinakamahinang color rendering ang sodium lamps, mas okay ang CFL and metal halide, pero sa choices na ito LED ang best answer.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Light Emitting Diode, o LED.
Sa mga choices na iyan, LED ang best answer for best CRI. U.S. DOE notes that quality LED bulbs offer comparable or better light quality, and typical CRI charts show standard LEDs commonly around 80+ and often much higher, while CFLs are typically lower, standard metal halide is generally lower than good LEDs, and high- and low-pressure sodium are the weakest in color rendering. (The Department of Energy's Energy.gov)
Bakit mali ang iba? Low-pressure sodium is notoriously terrible in color rendering. High-pressure sodium also has poor CRI compared with modern LEDs. Compact fluorescent is decent pero usually below good LED performance. Metal halide can be better than sodium, but generic standard metal-halide values are still usually below high-quality LED options in the references we found. Sa elimination, huwag ka agad mag-isip ng "HID must be stronger." Ang tinatanong ay CRI, hindi brightness or throw. For color rendering, the best answer here is LED. (Extension)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag CRI ang laban, pinakamahinang color rendering ang sodium lamps, mas okay ang CFL and metal halide, pero sa choices na ito LED ang best answer.
Sige, tuloy tayo.

question: Best and most practical for general lighting?

Ang clue dito ay "general lighting" at "most practical." Sa mga choices, ang compact fluorescent ang pinaka-bagay sa broad, everyday general illumination use. Philips describes compact fluorescent lamps as used for general downlighting in offices, retail, hospitals, and public buildings, and other lighting references describe fluorescent lamps as among the most useful and efficacious sources for general illumination. Samantalang ang low-pressure sodium at high-pressure sodium ay pang specialized outdoor applications dahil mahina ang color rendering, ang metal halide ay mas pang specialty/commercial/high-intensity use, at ang incandescent ay practical noon pero hindi na siya ang pinaka-practical sa efficiency at buhay ng lamp. Kaya sa reviewer logic, CFL ang natitira. (Philips lighting)
Elimination method:
Kapag nakita mo ang salitang general lighting, tanggalin mo muna ang specialized outdoor lamps like sodium and many HID choices. Tapos between incandescent and CFL, piliin mo ang mas practical at mas widely applicable sa general illumination. Iyon si Compact Fluorescent. (Philips lighting)

Kung ang tanong ay general lighting at wala sa choices ang LED, madalas Compact Fluorescent ang strongest practical answer.

question: Which of the following lamp has the WORST CRI?

Ito, sobrang importanteng keyword dito ang WORST CRI. Ang low-pressure sodium ay halos monochromatic light source. Kapag halos iisang color lang ang output mo, sobrang pangit ng color rendering mo. DOE and FEMP references describe low-pressure sodium as a very narrow-spectrum, essentially monochromatic source, kaya napakahina ng kanyang color rendering. Pareho silang poor sa color, pero ang low-pressure sodium ang pinaka-worst. (The Department of Energy's Energy.gov)

question: Which of the following is a BEST description of a CRI?

choices.text: Ability of a lamp to either enhance or ruin the true color of an object

answer: C. Ability of a lamp to either enhance or ruin the true color of an object
keyMemory: CRI = gaano katotoo o kalayo ang kulay ng object sa ilalim ng ilaw.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay ability of a lamp to either enhance or ruin the true color of an object.
Ang CRI o Color Rendering Index ay tungkol sa kung gaano ka-accurate ang paglabas ng kulay ng mga bagay sa ilalim ng isang light source compared with a reference source. DOE defines CRI as a measure of the degree of color shift objects undergo when illuminated by a light source compared with a reference. Kaya sa choices, ang pinakamalapit at pinakatamang description ay iyong nagsasabi na puwedeng ma-enhance o ma-ruin ng lamp ang true color appearance ng object. (The Department of Energy's Energy.gov)
Bakit mali ang iba?
Hindi CRI ang life expectancy, hindi rin siya simpleng color of an object, hindi rin power consumption, at hindi rin unit intensity. Lahat iyan ibang lighting metrics na. Kaya kapag nakita mo ang CRI, isipin mo agad ang salitang color fidelity or color accuracy. (The Department of Energy's Energy.gov)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
CRI = gaano katotoo o kalayo ang kulay ng object sa ilalim ng ilaw.

question: Which of the following is an ideal location for the generator?

question: Every time you turn on the faucet in the kitchen lavatory you noticed that the faucet vibrates and rattles before the water comes out. What do you think is the reason why this is happening?

choices.text: Too much pressure inside the pipe
There is no pressure inside the pipe
There is no air chamber installed.

answer: E. There is no air chamber installed.
keyMemory: Rattling pipes/faucet + shock sound = think water hammer, then think air chamber or arrester.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay There is no air chamber installed.
Ito ay classic water hammer/noisy pipes problem. Oatey explains that noisy or banging pipes happen because of hydraulic shock, and one traditional way to prevent or fix it is to provide an air chamber or other arresting device near the outlet so the pressure wave is absorbed. So kapag may rattling or vibrating sound around faucet operation, the reviewer-intended answer is the lack of an air chamber. (Oatey)
Bakit mali ang iba?
Too much pressure puwedeng aggravating factor, pero hindi iyon ang classic answer sa ganitong wording. No pressure obviously hindi tutulo ang water kung ganoon. Water runs too fast is descriptive but not the real device-related cause. No check valve is ibang issue. Ang tanong kasi humihingi ng pinaka-traditional plumbing answer for rattling and hydraulic shock, at iyon ay lack of an air chamber. (Oatey)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Rattling pipes/faucet + shock sound = think water hammer, then think air chamber or arrester.

question: Reference in buying commercially available plastic pipes?
keyMemory: Sa ganitong reviewer item, plastic pipe buying reference = Outside Diameter.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Outside Diameter.
Ito, magandang terminology question ito. Ang iniisip mo dapat dito ay: kapag bumibili ng plastic pipe, ano ang usual reference sa market sizing?
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa commercially available plastic pipes, ang mas common na reference ay outside diameter. Ibig sabihin, kapag tinatanong ang size ng pipe sa bentahan, madalas ang identifying basis ay OD, hindi simpleng kapal at hindi rin inside diameter.
Sa board exam, importante ang distinction na:
• may mga materials na nominal sizing ang gamit
• may mga plastic pipe systems na OD-based ang practical reference
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Thickness — mahalaga ito, oo, pero hindi ito ang pangunahing reference sa pagbili. More on specification ito, hindi first identifier.
c. Inside Diameter — puwedeng relevant sa flow capacity, pero hindi ito ang common commercial reference ng plastic pipe sizing sa ganitong reviewer item.
d. All of the given choices — trap ito. Totoo na puwedeng lahat ng iyan ay pinapansin sa specs, pero ang tanong ay reference in buying commercially available plastic pipes. Ang hinahanap ay pinaka-primary reference.
Elimination method
Ganito ka dapat mag-isip:
• Ang tanong ba ay specification detail o commercial size reference?
• Kapag commercial reference, hanapin mo ang pinaka-standard identifying dimension.
• Huwag ka agad sa "all of the above" kung may isang mas specific at mas technically correct na sagot.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay All of the given choices.
Maraming examinee ang nahuhulog diyan kapag lahat parang may katotohanan. Pero tandaan:
May difference ang relevant information sa primary buying reference.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ganitong reviewer item, plastic pipe buying reference = Outside Diameter.

question: What is the minimum air gap for faucets?
keyMemory: Minimum faucet air gap = 25 mm sa reviewer logic na ito.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 25 mm.
Ito ay classic plumbing sanitation question. Ang clue dito ay air gap.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang air gap ay protection laban sa backflow at backsiphonage. Sa ALE reviewer logic, ang minimum air gap for faucets ay 25 mm.
Bakit ito ang tama at dapat tandaan?
Kasi ang purpose ng air gap ay siguraduhin na hindi bumalik ang contaminated water sa potable supply. Kapag masyadong mababa ang outlet relative sa flood level rim, may contamination risk.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. 30 mm — mukhang plausible, pero hindi ito ang memorized reviewer figure dito.
c. 60 mm — masyadong mataas for the minimum asked.
d. 15 mm — too small, at delikado iyon bilang sanitary protection.
Elimination method
Kapag may tanong na minimum air gap, gawin mo ito:
• alisin ang masyadong mababa kung mukhang unsafe
• alisin din ang obviously excessive kung ang tanong ay minimum lang
• hanapin ang standard remembered plumbing value
Dito, ang strongest answer ay 25 mm.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay 30 mm, kasi mukhang mas "safe" at mas magandang rounded figure. Pero tandaan:
Board exam asks for standard answer, not your personal safety estimate.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Minimum faucet air gap = 25 mm sa reviewer logic na ito.

question: How can you determine if there is a leak in installed pipes?
keyMemory: Kapag installed pipes at leak detection ang tanong, ang formal answer ay Hydrostatic test.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Do a hydrostatic test.
Ito, importanteng practical plumbing question ito. Hindi sapat ang generic wording.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang hydrostatic test ang standard method para malaman kung may leak ang installed piping system by using water pressure and observing whether the system holds.
Bakit hindi lang "leak test"?
Kasi masyadong generic ang leak test. Totoo ang idea, pero hindi iyon ang precise technical procedure name na hinahanap ng tanong.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Do a leak test — too broad. Parang sinabi lang ulit ang purpose, hindi ang actual standard test name.
c. Do a hydraulic test — mukhang malapit, pero sa plumbing reviewer terminology, ang mas accepted term dito ay hydrostatic test.
d. Do a weep test — hindi ito ang standard answer para sa installed pipe leak determination.
Elimination method
Ganito ang tamang isip:
• Kapag generic ang isang choice at may mas exact technical term sa isa, piliin mo ang exact term.
• Between leak test and hydrostatic test, mas code- at reviewer-friendly si hydrostatic test.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay leak test, dahil iyon ang unang pumasok sa isip mo. Pero tandaan:
The board often wants the formal test name, not the plain-language description.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag installed pipes at leak detection ang tanong, ang formal answer ay Hydrostatic test.

question: Distance of a septic tank from any source of potable water should be?
keyMemory: Sa choices na ito, safe sanitary logic points to Beyond 15 meters.
explanation:
Ulit: sa choices na ibinigay, ang best answer ay Beyond 15 meters.
Dito gusto kong maging maingat. Ito iyong klase ng tanong na minsan ang reviewer choices hindi perfectly aligned sa mas conservative real-world sanitary planning values. Pero sa choices given, ang pinaka-defensible answer ay Beyond 15 meters.
Bakit ito ang tama sa choices?
Dahil ang general sanitary principle ay:
ang septic tank ay dapat malayo sa source of potable water para maiwasan ang contamination.
Sa apat na choices:
• within 15 m — mali agad ang direction
• 10 m — mas malapit pa
• 12 m — malapit pa rin
• beyond 15 m — ito lang ang clearly moving toward safer separation
Bakit kailangang maging maingat dito?
Sa actual practice, maraming references ang mas conservative pa kaysa 15 meters, depende sa source, soil, at local sanitary requirements. Pero dahil ito ay board-review item at iyan lang ang choices, Beyond 15 meters ang pipiliin mo.
Elimination method
Ganito ang isipin mo:
• Sanitary separation ba ito? Oo.
• So piliin mo ang pinakamalayo o at least ang choice na nagsasabing farther away.
• Huwag pipili ng "within 15 meters" dahil direction pa lang mali na.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay baka maghanap ka ng exact code number sa ulo mo at ma-paralyze ka.
Kapag ganun, gamitin mo ang sanitation logic:
Potable water source and septic tank should be well separated.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa choices na ito, safe sanitary logic points to Beyond 15 meters.

Ang trap dito ay urinal, kasi may mga urinal types na malilito ka. Pero kung gusto mo ng pinaka-stable, pinaka-sure answer, water closet ang matibay.

Water closet has an integral trap, so it does not need a separate P-trap.

question: It is a component of an electricity supply system that divides an electrical power feed into subsidiary circuits, while providing a protective fuse or circuit breaker for each circuit in a common enclosure.
keyMemory: Power feed divided into branch circuits with breakers in one enclosure = Distribution Board.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Distribution Board.
Ito ay halos textbook definition ng distribution board. Ang pinakamalaking clue dito ay:
• divides electrical power feed into subsidiary circuits
• protective fuse or circuit breaker for each circuit
• common enclosure
Iyan na iyan mismo ang ginagawa ng distribution board. Para itong central point kung saan hinahati ang incoming power papunta sa iba't ibang branch circuits, at bawat isa may sariling protection.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kasi ang distribution board ang may mga branch breaker o protective devices na nakalagay sa iisang enclosure. Sa building practice, ito ang panel na naghahati ng supply sa iba't ibang loads.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Switch Geard — mukhang ang intended term ay switchgear, pero even then, mas pang mas malalaki at more complex power-control/protection assemblies ito. Hindi ito ang pinaka-direct definition dito.
c. Switch Board — broad term ito, at puwedeng tumukoy sa switching/control panel, pero hindi ito ang pinaka-exact na definition na may individual fuse/circuit breaker for each subsidiary circuit.
d. Circuit — hindi ito enclosure or assembly; isa lang itong electrical path.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• May common enclosure ba sa definition? Oo.
• May multiple subsidiary circuits ba? Oo.
• May individual breaker/fuse per circuit ba? Oo.
Kapag ganyan, hanapin mo ang term na pang panel/distribution assembly, hindi single circuit lang.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay switchboard o switchgear dahil mukhang mas technical pakinggan. Pero tandaan:
kapag ang definition ay basic branch distribution with protective breakers in one enclosure, distribution board ang pinaka-exact.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Power feed divided into branch circuits with breakers in one enclosure = Distribution Board.

question: Which material would exhibit the highest sound absorption coefficient value which is best for home theaters and other related spaces?
keyMemory: Para sa home theater sound absorption, mas malakas ang polyester panel kaysa hard finish materials.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Polyester Panel.
Dito, ang clue ay highest sound absorption coefficient at best for home theaters. Ibig sabihin, ang hinahanap mo ay hindi matigas at reflective na material. Ang hinahanap mo ay material na porous, fibrous, and absorptive.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang polyester panel ay ginagamit talaga sa acoustic treatment dahil sound-absorbing siya. Mas bagay siya sa home theaters, media rooms, at other spaces na kailangan ng reverberation control.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Fiber Cement Board — matigas ito at mas reflective kaysa absorptive.
b. Solid Wood — may gamit ito sa acoustics in some tuning contexts, pero hindi ito ang highest absorber sa choices.
d. Polystyrene Board — insulation siya in many cases, pero hindi siya ang strongest sound absorber dito compared sa dedicated polyester acoustic panel.
Elimination method
Ganito ang dapat mong isipin:
• Hard and dense materials usually reflect sound
• Soft, fibrous, porous materials absorb sound
So:
• fiber cement board — tanggal
• solid wood — tanggal
• polystyrene — not the best among choices
• polyester panel — pinaka-acoustic talaga ang dating
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay solid wood, kasi maraming nag-a-associate ng wood sa "good acoustics." Oo, ginagamit ang wood sa acoustics, pero hindi ibig sabihin highest absorber agad siya. Minsan diffuser or reflective treatment pa nga ang function niya.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Para sa home theater sound absorption, mas malakas ang polyester panel kaysa hard finish materials.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: A collection of reflected sounds from the surfaces in an enclosure.
keyMemory: Collection of reflected sounds in a room = Reverberation.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Reverberation.
Ito ay basic acoustics terminology.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang reverberation ay ang persistence or collection of reflected sound sa loob ng isang enclosed space matapos tumigil ang original sound. Ibig sabihin, hindi isang isolated repeat lang, kundi halo-halong reflections from room surfaces.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Echo — mas distinct at delayed repetition ito ng sound. Iisang malinaw na balik, hindi general build-up of reflections.
c. Refraction — pagbaluktot ng wave dahil sa pagbabago ng medium or density, hindi ito reflected-sound build-up.
d. Diffraction — bending/spreading of waves around obstacles or openings.
Elimination method
Ito ang board logic:
• Kung maraming reflected sounds sa enclosure, isipin mo reverberation
• Kung isang malinaw na balik ng tunog, iyon ang echo
Common trap sa board exam
Maraming nalilito sa echo at reverberation.
Tandaan mo:
• Echo = distinct repeat
• Reverberation = lingering mixture of reflections
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Collection of reflected sounds in a room = Reverberation.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: It is a phone switching system that widely used in offices and plants, allowing a single access number to offer multiple lines to outside callers while providing a range of external lines to internal callers or staff.
keyMemory: Office/private telephone switching system = PBX.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay PBX.
Dito, gusto kong maging malinaw: PABX is closely related, at sa actual usage madalas napagpapalit sila sa reviewer context. Pero sa tanong na ito, ang mas general at mas tamang system name is PBX or Private Branch Exchange.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang description ay tungkol sa private telephone switching system used in offices and plants. Iyan ang classic description ng PBX.
Bakit hindi PABX?
Ang PABX ay basically automatic version or expanded form used in some contexts, pero kung ang tanong ay general office phone switching system, PBX ang mas umbrella at mas basic term.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. PABX — close siya, pero mas subtype/older term nuance ito kaysa pinaka-general system label here.
c. VoIP — communication technology/protocol ito, hindi exactly the switching system being defined in classic reviewer terms.
d. INTERNET — obviously hindi ito phone branch exchange.
Elimination method
Ganito ang isipin mo:
• Office internal phone switching ba?
• Multiple lines under one private exchange ba?
• Then think PBX first.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay dahil nauna na tayong nagkaroon ng tanong sa PABX, kaya baka automatic iyon ulit ang piliin mo. Pero pansinin mo:
• doon acronym expansion ang tanong
• dito system description ang tanong
So the more general answer is PBX.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Office/private telephone switching system = PBX.

question: It is a group of computers and associated devices that share a common communications line or wireless link to a server.
keyMemory: Group of computers sharing local communications resources = LAN.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay LAN.
Ito ay standard networking definition.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang LAN or Local Area Network ay group of computers and devices within a limited area sharing communications resources.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. WAN — wide area network ito, mas malawak geographic scope.
c. Internet — global network of networks ito, hindi specific local group sharing a common line to a server.
d. Ethernet — standard or technology for wired LAN connection, not the whole group/network definition itself.
Elimination method
Ganito:
• Group of computers in a local/common area? LAN
• Technology for wired connection? Ethernet
• Wide geographic coverage? WAN
Common trap sa board exam
The trap here is Ethernet because many people associate it with computer networking. Pero tandaan:
• LAN = network
• Ethernet = wired networking method/standard
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Group of computers sharing local communications resources = LAN.
________________________________________

answerConceptVisual.title: Key concept / value

question: It is the standard way to connect computers on a network over a wired connection.
keyMemory: Wired network connection standard = Ethernet.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Ethernet.
Dito baliktad naman ng previous item.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang Ethernet ang standard wired networking technology used to connect computers in a network.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. LAN — network type ito, not the actual wired connection standard.
b. WAN — wide network type ito, not wired connection standard.
c. Internet — global network ito, not the physical/data-link wired standard.
Elimination method
Ito ang mabilis na pair memory:
• LAN = local network
• Ethernet = wired way of connecting devices in that network
Common trap sa board exam
Kung minadali mo, puwede mong piliin ang LAN kasi network din naman ang tanong. Pero ang phrase na standard way to connect… over a wired connection is the giveaway for Ethernet.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Wired network connection standard = Ethernet.

visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: It is a networking device that forwards data packets between computer networks.
keyMemory: Packet forwarding between networks = Router.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Router.
Ito basic networking device identification.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang router ang device that forwards packets between networks.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Modem — converts signals for transmission over a communication medium, especially WAN/internet access contexts.
b. Repeater — nagpapalakas or nagre-regenerate ng signal, hindi nagde-decide ng path between networks.
c. Booster — generic signal-strengthening idea lang; hindi standard packet-forwarding answer.
Elimination method
Kapag tanong ay:
• forwards data packets
• between computer networks
automatic think router.
Common trap sa board exam
The trap is repeater because it also deals with signals/data. Pero tandaan:
• repeater = extends signal
• router = routes traffic between networks
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Packet forwarding between networks = Router.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: No visual assigned to avoid irrelevant image links.

question: It is a hardware device that converts data so that it can be transmitted from computer to computer over telephone wires.
keyMemory: Data conversion for telephone-line transmission = Modem.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Modem.
Ito ay classic old-school networking/telecom definition.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang modem ang nagmo-modulate at nagde-demodulate ng data para maipadala over telephone lines.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Repeater — signal regeneration lang, hindi data conversion for telephone transmission.
c. Booster — generic strengthening lang, not data conversion.
d. Router — packet forwarding device, not telephone-line signal conversion device.
Elimination method
Keyword spotting:
• telephone wires
• converts data
Kapag may ganiyang wording, modem agad.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay router dahil modern internet equipment din siya. Pero tandaan:
• router routes
• modem converts signal for transmission medium
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Data conversion for telephone-line transmission = Modem.

question: A type of Water closet which water enters through diagonal punching around the rim of the bowl, creating a vortex that draws the water down into the rear trap with a swirling action that scours the walls of the bowl.
keyMemory: Swirling rim action creating a vortex = Siphon Vortex.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Siphon Vortex.
Ito ay exact water-closet action terminology.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang pinakamalaking clue dito ay:
• diagonal punching around the rim
• creating a vortex
• swirling action
• scours the walls of the bowl
Kapag may salitang vortex at swirling action, halos ibinibigay na ang sagot: Siphon Vortex.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Reverse Trap — ibang closet type/action ito.
b. Siphon Jet — gumagamit ng jet action, not primarily vortex swirl around rim.
d. Washdown — washing action lang, not vortex-based scouring action.
Elimination method
Kapag definition question:
• if may jet, think siphon jet
• if may vortex/swirl, think siphon vortex
• if simple wash action, think washdown
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay siphon jet dahil may siphon din. Pero tandaan:
ang difference ay nasa how the water enters and acts.
Dito, swirling vortex ang clue.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Swirling rim action creating a vortex = Siphon Vortex.
