question: Water enters through an open rim, as though a bucket of water were dumped into the bowl, filling the front trapway and creating siphon action. This model provides quick removal of water with minimum water rise.
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay front trapway + siphon action + quick removal, think Reverse Trap.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Reverse Trap.
Ito medyo tricky kasi puwedeng malito ka sa washdown. Pero basahin mo nang mabuti.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang clue ay:
• open rim
• bucket of water were dumped
• filling the front trapway
• creating siphon action
• quick removal with minimum water rise
Iyong phrase na front trapway at specific siphonic action fits reverse trap better kaysa washdown.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Siphon Jet — dapat may jet action clue sana.
c. Siphon Vortex — dapat may rim swirl/vortex clue.
d. Washdown — washdown usually emphasizes washing contents through the trap, but the item specifically says creating siphon action and points to the front trapway, which pushes the answer toward reverse trap.
Elimination method
Ganito:
• may vortex ba? Wala. Tanggal siphon vortex.
• may jet ba? Wala. Tanggal siphon jet.
• natira: reverse trap vs washdown.
• may siphon action through front trapway ba? Oo.
• so reverse trap.
Common trap sa board exam
The trap is washdown, dahil sa "bucket dumped into bowl" image. Pero huwag ka titigil doon. Basahin mo ang susunod:
front trapway and siphon action.
Iyan ang naglalapit sa reverse trap.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay front trapway + siphon action + quick removal, think Reverse Trap.

question: Which of the following is best to use as refrigerant pipe?
keyMemory: Refrigerant piping = Copper Pipe sa standard HVAC reviewer logic.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Copper Pipe.
Ito ay very practical HVAC question.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa refrigeration at air-conditioning systems, copper pipe/tubing ang standard and most preferred dahil compatible siya sa refrigerant service, workable, durable, and widely used in the field.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Lead Pipe — hindi ito ginagamit for refrigerant piping.
b. Steel Pipe — may some uses sa certain heavy-duty systems, pero hindi ito ang most typical or best answer sa general reviewer item na ito.
d. Galvanized Iron Pipe — not the preferred standard answer for refrigerant lines.
Elimination method
Kapag AC/refrigeration piping ang tanong at may copper sa choices, malakas na malakas na sagot na iyan.
Common trap sa board exam
Minsan pipili ang examinee ng steel pipe dahil mas "matibay" ang dating. Pero tandaan:
ang tanong ay best to use as refrigerant pipe, at sa standard HVAC practice, copper ang go-to answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Refrigerant piping = Copper Pipe sa standard HVAC reviewer logic.

question: Which of the following needs an angle valve?

Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang angle valve ay shutoff/control valve commonly provided to fixture supply connections. Sa choices, ang hand spray ang pinaka-obvious fixture accessory na nangangailangan ng isolating valve.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Floor Drain — drainage fitting ito, hindi supply fixture requiring angle valve.

d. Water Hammer Arrester — device ito for shock absorption, not a fixture needing angle valve.

"Alin dito ang gumagamit ng water supply at may practical need for local shutoff?"

• hand spray — yes
Common trap sa board exam
The trap is sprinkler, kasi may tubig din naman. Pero fire sprinkler systems use different control arrangements, not this ordinary fixture shutoff logic.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Angle valve is commonly for fixture supply shutoff, kaya dito ang sagot ay Hand Spray.

question: Which of the following operates by detecting ionized particles in the air?
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay ionized particles in the air, isipin mo ionization smoke detector; sa choices na ito, sagot ay Smoke Detector.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Smoke Detector.
Dito gusto kong maging precise: ang most exact term sana ay ionization smoke detector. Pero dahil wala iyon sa choices, ang best answer among the given choices is Smoke Detector.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang detector type na gumagamit ng ionized particles/ionization principle ay under the category of smoke detector, specifically ionization-type smoke detector.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Photoelectric Detector — gumagamit ito ng light beam/light scattering principle, not ionization.
c. Temperature Detector — init ang basis, hindi particles in the air.
d. Flame Detector — detects flame radiation characteristics, not ionized airborne particles.
Elimination method
Ganito ang tamang isip:
• smoke ba ang related sa airborne particles? Oo.
• photoelectric ba ionization? Hindi.
• heat ba iyon? Hindi.
• flame ba iyon? Hindi.
So sa choices, Smoke Detector ang intended answer.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay photoelectric detector, dahil smoke detector din ang dating niya sa isip ng iba. Pero tandaan:
• photoelectric = light principle
• ionization = smoke detector category, specifically ionization smoke detector
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay ionized particles in the air, isipin mo ionization smoke detector; sa choices na ito, sagot ay Smoke Detector.

question: Identify
keyMemory: Sa trap identification, huwag puro shape lang ang tingnan. Tingnan mo rin ang orientation at installation context. Dito, Running Trap ang tama.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Running Trap.
Yung image ay hindi mukhang ordinary U-trap under a lavatory. Mukha itong trap installed in line with a horizontal run, with horizontal inlet and outlet and trap body below — classic running trap silhouette.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang running trap ay installed in a horizontal drainage line. Sa image:
• may horizontal run from left to right
• may trap body forming a U below
• hindi siya iyong usual simple fixture U-trap look
• hindi rin vertical S-trap arrangement
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. U-Trap — usually mas direct fixture-trap form ito, not this in-line drain-line arrangement.
b. S-Trap — dapat may S-like vertical drop/outlet relationship, which the image does not show.
d. Pipe Trap — too generic at hindi ito ang specific recognizable trap form dito.
Elimination method
Kapag image identification ng traps:
• S-trap = vertical drop with S form
• U-trap = simple U form, often fixture-associated
• running trap = in-line horizontal drain trap
Tingnan mo kung saan pumapasok at lumalabas ang pipe.
Dito, horizontal line trap ang dating, kaya running trap.
Common trap sa board exam
The trap here is U-trap, kasi may U shape sa baba. Pero huwag ka tumingin lang sa baba. Tingnan mo ang buong arrangement ng inlet at outlet. Iyon ang maglalabas na running trap ito, hindi simpleng U-trap lang.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa trap identification, huwag puro shape lang ang tingnan. Tingnan mo rin ang orientation at installation context. Dito, Running Trap ang tama.
Sige, tuloy tayo.

question: A tube, pipe or duct for enclosing and protecting electric wires or cable?
keyMemory: Conduit is a type of raceway. Kapag broad protective enclosure ang definition, sagot ay Raceway.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Raceway.
Ito, maganda ang distinction na ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang raceway ang broader technical term for an enclosed channel, tube, pipe, or duct used to enclose and protect conductors or cables.
Ang conduit ay isang type of raceway.
Bakit hindi conduit?
Kung ang choice set ay may raceway at conduit, at ang tanong ay broad enough to include tube, pipe or duct, mas exact umbrella term si raceway.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Cable tray — support system ito, usually open, not enclosed protective tube/duct.
Duct — too generic.
Conduit — close, pero mas specific subtype lang.
Elimination method
Kapag may broad definition at may umbrella term sa choices, piliin ang umbrella term.
Dito:
• broad = raceway
• specific subtype = conduit

question: It is an electrical apparatus that provides emergency power to a load when the input power source or main power fails.
keyMemory: Emergency power when main fails = UPS.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Uninterruptible Power Supply, o UPS.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang UPS ang device that provides emergency power immediately when the main source fails.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
AVR — voltage stabilizer ito, not emergency backup power source.
Generator — backup source siya, oo, pero hindi immediate no-interruption device in this exact definition.
Surge protector — protects against spikes, not power failure continuity.
Elimination method
Tandaan mo ang pair:
• UPS = instant/continuous emergency backup
• Generator = alternate source but not the same exact apparatus definition here
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Emergency power when main fails = UPS.

Ang surge protector ang talagang designed to protect equipment from voltage spikes or surges.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
AVR — regulates voltage, pero hindi iyon ang pinaka-direct anti-spike protection device asked here.
UPS — backup power device muna siya.
Generator — alternate power source, not spike protector.
Elimination method
Keyword spotting:
• voltage spikes
• protect devices
Kapag ganiyan, huwag ka nang lumayo sa salitang surge.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?

question: It is a static electrical device that transfers electrical energy between two or more circuit through electromagnetic induction.
keyMemory: Electromagnetic induction between circuits = Transformer.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Transformer.
Ito very direct definition ito. Ang phrase na "transfers electrical energy between two or more circuits through electromagnetic induction" ay halos textbook definition na ng transformer. Iyan mismo ang trabaho niya: maglipat ng electrical energy from one circuit to another by electromagnetic induction. (Schneider Electric)
Bakit mali ang iba? UPS gives backup power kapag nawalan ng main source, hindi iyon induction-transfer device. Generator creates electrical power from mechanical energy. Surge protector protects against voltage spikes. So ang clue talaga ay electromagnetic induction. Kapag iyan ang nakita mo, alisin mo lahat ng protection at backup-power devices. Matitira si Transformer. (Schneider Electric)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Electromagnetic induction between circuits = Transformer.

question: Which of the following needs an individual circuit breaker?
keyMemory: Kapag pinapili ka ng pinaka-obvious na dedicated circuit load, window-type AC ang malakas na ALE answer.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Window Type Air-Conditioning System.
Dito, ang pinaka-board-logical answer ay ang window-type air-conditioning system dahil room air conditioners, especially larger units, commonly require a dedicated circuit. U.S. DOE guidance says larger room air conditioners need a dedicated circuit, and reviewer logic usually treats AC units as the clearest "individual breaker" load among household/appliance choices. (The Department of Energy's Energy.gov)
Bakit natatanggal ang iba? Flat iron at microwave are heavy appliances, oo, pero sa reviewer setup ang pinaka-obvious fixed dedicated load is air-conditioning. Washing machine can also be given dedicated supply in many designs, pero sa ganitong multiple choice, ang may pinakamalinaw na "sariling breaker" identity ay window AC dahil may motor-compressor starting load at tuloy-tuloy na significant current draw. Kaya sa elimination, hanapin mo iyong fixed, motorized, high-load appliance. (The Department of Energy's Energy.gov)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag pinapili ka ng pinaka-obvious na dedicated circuit load, window-type AC ang malakas na ALE answer.

question: It is part of an electric distribution network. An electrical wiring circuit in a building which carries power from a transformer or switch gear to a distribution panel.
keyMemory: Transformer/switchgear to distribution panel = Feeder line.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Feeder line.
Ito definition item ulit. Ang feeder or feeder line is the conductor or circuit carrying power from one distribution point to another, such as from switchgear or service equipment toward a distribution panel. PEC-style definitions also place feeders between the source/service side and the final branch-circuit overcurrent device. (jadelearning.com)
Bakit mali ang iba? Service lateral is underground utility service. Service drop is overhead utility service. Service entrance is the service conductors/equipment entry side. Pero ang tanong ay hindi utility-to-building entry; ang tanong ay from transformer or switchgear to a distribution panel. Iyan ang feeder function. Kaya kapag nakita mo ang idea na internal distribution from one major point to another, think feeder line. (jadelearning.com)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Transformer/switchgear to distribution panel = Feeder line.

question: It is an object or type of material that allows the flow of electrical current in one or more directions.
keyMemory: Allows current to flow = Conductor.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Conductors.
Ang pinaka-keyword dito ay "allows the flow of electrical current." Iyan ang literal na definition ng electrical conductor. Conductors are materials that allow electricity to flow through them. (TechTarget)
Bakit mali ang iba? Insulators kabaligtaran niyan. Wires at cables can be made of conductors, pero hindi sila ang pinaka-basic material/category definition na hinahanap ng tanong. Ibig sabihin, mas broad at mas foundational ang conductors. Ito ang iniisip mo dapat: kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa property ng material, huwag pumili ng specific product form like wire or cable. (TechTarget)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Allows current to flow = Conductor.

Bakit mali ang iba? Wire at cable are conductors, yes, pero hindi sila iyong rigid strip/bar arrangement na nasa definition. (Wärtsilä)

question: If there is 8 staff in an office? How many voice and data outlet needed if the ratio of staff for computer & telephone is 1:1?
keyMemory: Kapag 1:1 ang voice at data per staff, then one person = one voice + one data.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 8 voice and 8 data.
Pure ratio lang ito. Kung 8 staff at ang ratio for computer and telephone is 1:1, ibig sabihin bawat staff may 1 data outlet at 1 voice outlet. So total mo: 8 voice at 8 data.
Bakit mali ang iba? 4 and 4 kulang. 8 voice and data malabo at incomplete ang bilang. 16 voice and data puwedeng mapili kung mamadaliin mo at iisipin mong total 16 na outlets, pero ang tanong ay hinahati sa voice at data. Kaya mas exact ang 8 voice and 8 data.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag 1:1 ang voice at data per staff, then one person = one voice + one data.

question: As per Fire Code of the Philippines, how many sprinkler heads are needed for an office in a 4 storey office building?
keyMemory: Sa office/business occupancy, bantayan mo ang 15-meter height trigger, hindi basta number of storeys lang.
explanation:
Ulit: sa choices na ito, ang best answer ay 0.
Dito gusto kong maging maingat. Sa Fire Code of the Philippines IRR, for business occupancy, approved supervised sprinkler protection is required throughout when the building is 15 meters or more in height. The question gives you only "4-storey office building" and does not say it reaches 15 meters. So in ALE reviewer logic, the intended answer is usually 0, because the trigger given in the code is height, not simply "four storeys" for business occupancy. (VIZCODE)
Ito ang common trap: baka madala ka sa ibang occupancy rules na may "4 storeys or more" wording. Meron kasing ibang occupancies sa Fire Code na ganyan ang trigger. Pero office = business occupancy, and the cited provision says 15 meters or more in height. Kaya kung ang tanong ay simpleng "4-storey office building" lang, hindi sapat iyon by itself to conclude sprinkler requirement. Kaya 0 ang best reviewer answer. This is an inference from the Fire Code trigger. (VIZCODE)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa office/business occupancy, bantayan mo ang 15-meter height trigger, hindi basta number of storeys lang.
________________________________________

question: What is the minimum wire size of an Air conditioning unit for an office?
keyMemory: Sa ganitong generic reviewer item, AC unit minimum wire size = 5.5 mm² ang usual expected answer.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot dito ay 5.5 mm².
Dito, honest tayo: in real design, exact wire size depends on the actual AC load, current, voltage, and length of run. Pero sa reviewer-style item na ganito, 5.5 mm² is the usual expected answer. Philippine plan/spec examples commonly show AC units on 5.5 mm² THWN, and reviewer references also land common AC-unit sizing in that choice set at 5.5 mm². (Scribd)
Paano mag-eliminate? 2.0 mm² at 3.5 mm² usually feel too light for the "air-conditioning unit for an office" wording. 8.0 mm² sounds oversized for a basic reviewer minimum unless the unit is clearly much larger. Kaya ang natitira, and the usual exam answer, is 5.5 mm². So ito iyong tanong na hindi mo sasagutin by perfect design computation; sasagutin mo siya by reviewer convention plus common local specification practice. (Scribd)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ganitong generic reviewer item, AC unit minimum wire size = 5.5 mm² ang usual expected answer.

question: Best medium for the internet connection for an office?
keyMemory: Best office internet medium = Fiber Optic Cable.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Fiber Optic Cable.
Ang clue dito ay best medium for office internet connection. Sa modern office connectivity, fiber optic is generally favored for higher speed, better reliability, lower latency, and stronger scalability than copper-based options like UTP, STP, or coaxial. (Lenovo)
Bakit mali ang iba? UTP at STP are very useful inside structured cabling runs, yes, pero kung "best medium for internet connection" ang tanong, mas mataas ang performance ceiling ni fiber. Coaxial has its place, but it is not the best answer among these choices for office-grade internet backbone thinking. Kaya ang board strategy dito: kapag tinanong ang best medium, huwag piliin ang pinaka-common local copper run; piliin ang highest-performance transmission medium. (Lenovo)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Best office internet medium = Fiber Optic Cable.

question: How many TR are needed for a 150 sqm office?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer thumb rule, 150 m² office ≈ 3 TR.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 3 TR.
Dito reviewer thumb-rule ang ginagamit, hindi full cooling-load calculation. A common preliminary HVAC thumb rule is about 1 ton per 500 square feet. Ang 150 m² is about 1,615 ft². Divide mo iyan by 500 ft²/TR, lalabas ka sa about 3.2 TR, so the nearest choice is 3 TR. (www.cedengineering.com)
Ito ang dapat mong bantayan: huwag agad pumili ng 5 TR dahil "office" ang word. Ang tanong ay hindi detailed design; reviewer estimate ito. So ang elimination mo:
• 1 TR, obviously kulang for 150 m² office
• 7 TR, sobra sa rough rule
• between 3 and 5, mas malapit ang 3 TR sa rule-of-thumb estimate
At tandaan, this is only a preliminary estimate, not a substitute for detailed load calc. (www.cedengineering.com)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer thumb rule, 150 m² office ≈ 3 TR.

question: What is the minimum wire size of a Service Drop if uses multi conductor?
keyMemory: Sa PEC reviewer logic: service entrance/service drop in multiconductor cable = 8.0 mm² minimum.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 8 mm².
Ito may direct Philippine Electrical Code basis. PEC says service-entrance conductors shall not be smaller than 14 mm² unless in multiconductor cable, and multiconductor cable shall not be smaller than 8.0 mm². Related service provisions also state a minimum of 8.0 mm² copper for service-drop conductors in the cited provisions. So with the key phrase "if uses multi conductor," the intended answer is 8 mm². (Filipino Engineer)
Paano mag-eliminate? Kapag may nakita kang multiconductor, huwag kang basta kukuha ng general minimum tulad ng 14 mm². Iyan ang trap. Ang tanong is telling you na may exception/alternate minimum dahil multiconductor cable ang gamit. Kaya ang natitira ay 8 mm², not 14, and definitely not 5.5 or 3.5. (Filipino Engineer)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa PEC reviewer logic: service entrance/service drop in multiconductor cable = 8.0 mm² minimum.

question: It is a fast-acting circuit breaker designed to shut off electric power in the event of a ground-fault within as little as 1/40 of a second. Which ideal for toilet and kitchen?
keyMemory: Kitchen/toilet ground-fault protection = GFCI.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay GFCI.
Ito halos verbatim na sa OSHA description. A GFCI, or ground-fault circuit interrupter, is a fast-acting protective device designed to shut off power in as little as 1/40 of a second when a ground fault occurs, and it is especially appropriate for wet or damp locations like kitchens and toilets. (osha.gov)
Bakit mali ang iba? NEMA is a standards organization/classification context, not this device. UPS is backup power equipment. GFRC is not the intended electrical-protection answer here. Kapag nakita mo ang clues na ground-fault, 1/40 second, at wet areas, huwag ka nang lumayo sa GFCI. (osha.gov)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kitchen/toilet ground-fault protection = GFCI.

explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay DVI.
Ito medyo malinaw ang connector image. Mukha itong DVI connector.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Makikita sa image na:
• malapad ang connector body
• may screw points sa sides
• rectangular pin area ang form
Iyan ang classic look ng DVI.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
HDMI — mas maliit at mas flat ang shape.
DP o DisplayPort — ibang edge/profile ang connector.
VGA — D-sub shape iyon, hindi ganito.
Elimination method
Kapag image ID ng connectors:
• VGA = D-sub shape with many visible pin holes
• HDMI/DP = slimmer digital connectors
• DVI = bigger rectangular connector with side screws
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay VGA, dahil pareho silang old monitor connectors. Pero tingnan mo:
VGA is trapezoidal/D-sub form.
DVI is more rectangular and broader.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Wide rectangular monitor connector with screw mounts = DVI.

question: Identify
keyMemory: Valve with sensing bulb/capillary = Expansion Valve.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Expansion Valve.
Obvious typo man ang "Angel Valve," pero hindi iyon ang tama rito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Yung image ay may capillary tube/sensing bulb assembly attached to the valve body — napakalakas na clue ito ng expansion valve, especially in refrigeration and air-conditioning systems.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Angel Valve — likely typo for angle valve, pero angle valve does not have that sensing-bulb/capillary setup.
Globe Valve — ibang valve form at function ito; walang ganiyang sensing line.
Copper Valve — not a standard specific valve type answer.
Elimination method
Kapag may image ng valve with:
• body
• outlet/inlet
• sensing bulb/capillary tube
think expansion valve agad.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay globe valve, kasi valve body din siya. Pero ang pinakamahalagang clue ay iyong capillary/sensing element, at iyan ay hindi globe valve feature.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Valve with sensing bulb/capillary = Expansion Valve.

question: Identify
keyMemory: Kapag wall-mounted temperature control device ang image, ang safest answer ay Thermostat.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Thermostat.
Ito, image-based identification ito. Yung device sa picture ay mukhang wall-mounted control unit na may display at may up/down buttons, kasama pa iyong word na HOLD, which is very common sa thermostat controls.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang thermostat ang ginagamit para mag-set at mag-control ng room temperature.
Kapag may wall device ka na:
• may screen
• may control buttons
• may hold or setting function
malakas na malakas ang possibility na thermostat iyan.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Biometric Machine — hindi ito access-control or fingerprint device ang itsura.
c. Thermometer — thermometer only measures temperature; usually hindi ganyan ang control-style device with hold button.
d. Humidifier Control — possible in some systems, pero sa choices, mas direct at mas common ang thermostat.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Measuring lang ba ito, o control device?
Mukhang control device, hindi simpleng pang-sukat lang.
• May wall-control interface ba? Oo.
• So tanggal ang thermometer.
• Hindi rin ito biometric.
• Between humidifier control and thermostat, mas common at mas exact ang thermostat.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay thermometer, kasi may temperature kaagad sa isip mo.
Pero tandaan:
thermometer = measure only
thermostat = measure plus control
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag wall-mounted temperature control device ang image, ang safest answer ay Thermostat.

question: In a "Samgyupsal" Restaurant, what should you provide to be at least minimize or to prevent the flooring from being too slippery?
keyMemory: Sa greasy, smoky food spaces, proper exhaust ang pinaka-importanteng first-line design solution.
explanation:
Ulit: ang best answer dito ay Enough Exhaust Fan.
Dito, practical building-utilities logic ang gamitin mo. Sa samgyupsal restaurant, ang major issue ay:
• smoke
• grease-laden vapor
• heat
• oil particles
Kapag hindi maayos ang exhaust, kumakapit ang greasy vapor sa surfaces, including floors, kaya nagiging madulas.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang exhaust system ang pinaka-direct utility solution para alisin ang:
• usok
• mantika sa hangin
• cooking vapor
• odor and heat buildup
So kung ang target mo ay minimize the cause of slippery conditions, hindi lang remedy, ang sagot ay Enough Exhaust Fan.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Enough Aircon — comfort ang tinutulungan nito, hindi grease removal.
c. Enough Lighting — visibility lang ang naibibigay nito, hindi niya inaalis ang cause ng slippery floor.
d. Enough Cleaners — maintenance solution ito, hindi building-utilities solution.
e. Enough Service Crew — manpower solution ito, hindi environmental-control solution.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Ang tanong ba ay tungkol sa root cause control o operational response?
• Since building utilities reviewer ito, hanapin mo ang mechanical/environmental control answer.
• Doon papasok ang Exhaust Fan.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay Enough Cleaners, kasi practical naman talaga iyon sa totoong buhay.
Pero tandaan:
board exam often wants the design/system answer, not the staffing answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa greasy, smoky food spaces, proper exhaust ang pinaka-importanteng first-line design solution.

question: Which of the following needs an exhaust fan?

explanation:

Dito gusto kong maging honest: sa actual practice, several toilet-related areas can benefit from exhaust ventilation, especially shower areas for moisture and water closet areas for odor control. Pero kung one best answer lang ang hahanapin sa reviewer logic, pinaka-intended dito ang Water Closet Cubicle dahil iyon ang pinaka-directly associated with odor removal in enclosed toilet spaces.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang exhaust fan is commonly associated with enclosed toilet areas to remove:
• odor
• stale air
• humidity
• contaminants
Sa listed options, ang water closet cubicle ang pinaka-direct at pinaka-classic na space na inaasahang i-exhaust.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Urinal Area — puwede rin sa actual design, pero less "single best answer" than WC cubicle in reviewer logic.
c. Shower Area — moisture control yes, but the most classic exhaust-fan answer in sanitary spaces is still enclosed WC area.
d. Lavatory Area — not the most direct answer.
e. Hand Spray — fixture ito, hindi area.
Elimination method
Ganito ka dapat mag-isip:
• Hanapin ang pinaka-odorous and enclosed sanitation space
• Huwag pumili ng fixture
• Huwag pumili ng less critical area if there is a stronger odor-control candidate
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay shower area, kasi iniisip mo agad moisture. Tama naman ang concern, pero reviewer-type question ito, at ang mas classic answer ay WC cubicle.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa toilet-area questions, exhaust fan is most strongly associated with the water closet cubicle.

Sa reviewer logic, the brush along the skirting functions as a safety deflector.

• brush siya
• safety-related ang purpose niya

• nasa escalator side/skirt
• brush form
• safety purpose
then hanapin ang choice with safety + directional/protective function.

Ang trap dito ay Safety Interceptor, kasi parang technical din pakinggan.
Pero the brush is more about deflecting away, not "intercepting" in the usual wording.

question: In a hospital, what should you provided to avoid power interruption during operations?
keyMemory: Critical no-break power = UPS.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay UPS.
Napaka-importanteng keyword dito ang avoid power interruption.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang UPS or Uninterruptible Power Supply gives immediate backup power with virtually no interruption.
Sa hospital operations, lalo na sa critical procedures, iyon ang kailangan mo.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. AVR — stabilizes voltage, but does not maintain power during outage.
c. ATS — automatic transfer switch transfers to backup source, but there can still be transfer time; hindi ito ang pinaka-direct no-interruption answer.
d. Router — unrelated.
Elimination method
Ito ang pair na dapat mong tandaan:
• UPS = no-interruption backup
• ATS = transfer mechanism
• AVR = voltage stabilizer
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay ATS, kasi backup-power related din siya.
Pero ang tanong ay avoid interruption, so ang mas exact answer ay UPS.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Critical no-break power = UPS.

question: In a Milk Tea Shop, ideally, which of the following lighting luminaire should be used if the owner wants to highlight the Menu Board,

choices.text: Pendant Light with Mercury Vapor Lamp
Spot light with Metal Halide Bulb

answer: C. Eyeball Light with Halogen Bulb
keyMemory: For small interior display/menu highlight, adjustable accent light ang sagot — dito, Eyeball Light with Halogen Bulb.
explanation:
Ulit: ang best answer dito ay Eyeball Light with Halogen Bulb.
Dito accent lighting ang logic. Ang key phrase ay highlight the menu board.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang eyeball light is adjustable/directional, kaya maganda siya for aiming light toward a vertical feature like a menu board.
Ang halogen bulb also gives crisp accent lighting and good color rendering in classic reviewer logic.
Bakit hindi spot light with metal halide?
Puwede rin sana sa ibang display situations, pero for a smaller interior retail application like a milk tea shop menu board, the more practical and reviewer-friendly interior accent answer is eyeball light with halogen bulb.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Track Light with HPS lamp — HPS has poor color rendering; not ideal for interior menu-board highlighting.
b. Pendant Light with Mercury Vapor Lamp — wrong mood and wrong lamp logic for menu-board accent.
d. Downlight with LED Bulb — too general; not as targeted for highlighting a board.
e. Spot light with Metal Halide Bulb — strong contender, but more likely overkill versus the classic small-shop accent answer.
Elimination method
Ganito ang tamang isip:
• Targeted accent ba ang kailangan? Oo.
• So alisin ang broad general lighting like downlight.
• Alisin ang poor-color-rendering or wrong-use lamps like HPS and mercury vapor.
• Between remaining directional accent choices, piliin ang mas maliit-shop interior solution: eyeball light with halogen bulb.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay spot light with metal halide bulb, kasi accent din iyon.
Pero sa maliit na interior menu-board highlighting, the more classic reviewer answer is eyeball light with halogen.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For small interior display/menu highlight, adjustable accent light ang sagot — dito, Eyeball Light with Halogen Bulb.

question: …Circuit no. 8 has 2 convenience outlets. Circuit no. 9 is for the range. Circuit no. 10 is for the existing ref. Circuit no. 11 and 12 are spare. What circuit no. should you connect to the additional ref?

choices.text: Circuit no. 10, both ref should be on the same circuit

keyMemory: Kapag may major appliance at may spare circuit, huwag mo isiksik sa convenience outlets. Gamitin ang spare dedicated circuit.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Circuit no. 11.
Ito, practical electrical planning logic ito. Refrigerators are commonly treated as loads that are best placed on their own dedicated circuit, or at the very least not mixed with already occupied convenience-outlet circuits when you still have spare circuits available. Since No. 11 and 12 are spare, and No. 10 is already serving the existing refrigerator, the cleanest and safest reviewer answer is Circuit no. 11. (Parker Electric)
Bakit mali ang iba? Circuit 8 is already a convenience-outlet circuit, so hindi iyan ang ideal place for another refrigerator. Any of 1–7 mas lalong hindi, kasi loaded convenience-outlet circuits na ang mga iyon. Circuit 10 is tempting, pero dalawang refrigerator sa iisang existing ref circuit is poor practice if may spare ka naman. Sa ALE logic, kapag may spare dedicated circuit, doon mo ilalagay ang added appliance. (Parker Electric)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may major appliance at may spare circuit, huwag mo isiksik sa convenience outlets. Gamitin ang spare dedicated circuit.

question: Which of the following is best to be used for equipment/appliances for kitchens and/or toilets
keyMemory: Kapag kitchen o toilet ang tanong, isipin mo agad GFCI. Kung typo ang choice, sundan mo ang intended protective device.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot, as intended, ay GFRC — pero malinaw na typo ito for GFCI.
Dito, obvious na ang intended answer ng item ay GFCI kahit ang naka-print ay GFRC. Kitchens and toilets are wet areas, and the protective device specifically used there to reduce shock hazard is the Ground-Fault Circuit Interrupter. Multiple electrical-safety sources, including Philippine-oriented reviewer materials and OSHA-style descriptions, consistently identify GFCI as the right protection for wet locations. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba? NEMA/MEMA is not the protective device being asked. Circuit breaker is too general; it protects against overcurrent, not specifically ground-fault shock in wet areas. UPS is backup power equipment. Kaya kahit mali ang printing ng choice, the only defensible intended answer is GFCI/GFRC as printed. (Studocu)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag kitchen o toilet ang tanong, isipin mo agad GFCI. Kung typo ang choice, sundan mo ang intended protective device.

question: Which of the following doesn't need an angle valve?
keyMemory: Kapag angle valve ang usapan, isipin mo fixture water-supply shutoff, hindi drain/electrical appliance.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Waste Disposal Unit.
Ito ang logic: ang angle valve is a local shutoff valve for a water-supplied fixture or attachment. Bidet sprays and toilet handheld sprays are commonly installed with angle valves or T-valves/shutoff valves. A waste disposal unit, on the other hand, is primarily a drain/electrical appliance under the sink, not the sort of end fixture that is normally identified by needing an angle valve in this reviewer context. (images.thdstatic.com)
Bakit mali ang iba? Bidet at Hand Spray are the easiest to eliminate because angle valves are commonly used with them. Bain-marie is not the strongest answer because it can still involve controlled supply connections depending on the setup. So among the choices, the one that most clearly does not need an angle valve is Waste Disposal Unit. (jaquar.it)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag angle valve ang usapan, isipin mo fixture water-supply shutoff, hindi drain/electrical appliance.

question: What should be the minimum size of the wire for convenience outlet if it is intended for charging cellphone while playing Mobile Legends.
keyMemory: Sa reviewer logic, convenience outlet = 3.5 mm² ang usual minimum answer.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 3.5 mm².
Mukhang biro ang "while playing Mobile Legends," pero ang tanong ay tungkol pa rin sa convenience outlet circuit, hindi sa actual liit ng cellphone charger load. Sa reviewer/PEC-style wiring examples, convenience outlets are commonly assigned 3.5 mm² copper conductors. So kahit maliit ang actual gadget load, ang sinusunod mo ay minimum branch-circuit wiring practice for the outlet, hindi charger rating. (electricaldesign1.blogspot.com)
Bakit mali ang iba? 2.0 mm² is too light for the usual reviewer answer for convenience outlets. 5.5 mm² and 8.0 mm² are oversized for this context. Ang trap dito ay mapatawa ka sa Mobile Legends at isipin mong puwede na ang manipis na wire; pero ang wiring is sized by the outlet circuit standard, hindi by one temporary gadget. (electricaldesign1.blogspot.com)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer logic, convenience outlet = 3.5 mm² ang usual minimum answer.

question: Which of the following devices should you use if you want to control the lighting intensity of your living room without appreciably affecting spatial distribution?

explanation:

Ito halos textbook definition na mismo ng dimmer. A dimmer changes light output or intensity without materially changing the spatial distribution of the luminaire. The exact wording appears in lighting references: a light dimmer varies the light intensity without appreciably affecting spatial distribution. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba? Photoelectric switch responds to ambient light automatically; hindi iyan ang ginagamit mo para manu-manong i-dim ang living room. Mood switch is not a standard technical term. Light regulator sounds descriptive, but dimmer switch is the exact recognized device. Sa board exam, kapag may exact technical term versus generic wording, piliin mo ang exact term. (PacLights)

question: A Trap used between House Drain and House Sewer?
keyMemory: Between house drain and house sewer = Running Trap sa reviewer/plumbing logic.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Running Trap.
A running trap, or house trap in older terminology, is installed along the building drainage line outside or near the building to prevent sewer gases from entering the structure. That is why, in reviewer logic, the trap between the house drain and the house sewer is the running trap. (Balkan Sewer & Water Main Service)
Bakit mali ang iba? P-trap and bottle trap are fixture-side traps. Gooseneck is not the standard answer for this main-line location. Ito ang board strategy: kapag main line / house drain to house sewer ang tanong, huwag kang pumunta sa sink- or lavatory-type traps. Piliin ang trap used along the drainage line itself. (Civil Engineering Portal)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Between house drain and house sewer = Running Trap sa reviewer/plumbing logic.

Sa elimination, kapag nakita mo ang phrase na public establishments, think flushometer. (ASPE)

question: In an open-ceiling-slab-soffit-designed Siomai House. What needs to be done on the ceiling to minimize the noise coming from the customer?
keyMemory: Kapag open ceiling + noisy space, think ceiling clouds for sound absorption.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Provide Ceiling Clouds.
Ceiling clouds are suspended acoustic absorbers specifically used to reduce echo and reverberation, especially in open-ceiling environments where you do not have a conventional full ceiling. That makes them the strongest answer for an open slab-soffit food space with noisy customers. (acousticalsolutions.com)
Bakit mali ang iba? High gloss paint makes surfaces more reflective. Textured paint alone is too weak compared with a true absorptive treatment. Carpet flooring helps floor reflections but the question specifically asks what to do on the ceiling. Polystyrene on the wall is not the best direct ceiling solution in this item. Kaya ang keyword na bantayan mo rito ay open ceiling — iyan ang clue for ceiling clouds. (acousticsfirst.com)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag open ceiling + noisy space, think ceiling clouds for sound absorption.

question: To minimize the sound transmission of a Function Hall. What needs to be done?

choices.text: Change the chairs to fully upholstered fabric

keyMemory: Sound transmission = think sealing leaks and gaps, not just adding soft finishes.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Seal all the gaps.
This is about sound transmission, not just echo inside the room. One of the most basic and effective sound-isolation strategies is to seal gaps, cracks, joints, and penetrations, because sound leaks through air paths very easily. Acoustic guidance repeatedly emphasizes sealing every gap to reduce sound transmission. (gypsum.org)
Bakit mali ang iba? Upholstered chairs, ceiling clouds, and fabric help mostly with absorption and room acoustics, not primary sound leakage between spaces. More air-conditioning units has nothing to do with sound isolation. Ito ang malaking trap: maraming acoustics questions ang nalilito sa absorption versus transmission. Dito, ang target ay transmission, so the best answer is the one that closes leakage paths: seal all the gaps. (Acoustical Surfaces)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sound transmission = think sealing leaks and gaps, not just adding soft finishes.

question: To enhance the acoustic property of a Function Hall. What needs to be done?

choices.text: use more hard and reflective material
use more absorptive and texture material

answer: D. use more absorptive and texture material
keyMemory: Better room acoustics = more absorptive, less reflective surfaces.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay use more absorptive and texture material.
To improve acoustics in a function hall, you generally want materials that absorb sound and reduce unwanted reflections and reverberation. Acoustic textiles and absorptive treatments are widely used for exactly that purpose, and textured/coarse surfaces help break up reflections rather than bounce them back harshly. (Ljuds)
Bakit mali ang iba? Hard and reflective material usually worsens reverberation. Expensive finishes is meaningless acoustically unless the finish itself is absorptive. Use more fabric is not bad, but absorptive and textured material is more complete and more technically precise. More uneven and plain is self-contradictory and weakly worded. Kaya sa elimination, piliin ang most acoustically specific option, not the most decorative-sounding one. (StudySmarter UK)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Better room acoustics = more absorptive, less reflective surfaces.

question: Method of joining MDPE, HDPE and other plastic pipes using special fittings that have built-in electric heating elements which are used to weld the joint together.
keyMemory: Built-in heating element fittings for HDPE/MDPE = Electrofusion.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Electro Fusion.
This is an exact definition of electrofusion. The method uses special fittings with built-in electric heating elements that melt and fuse the fitting and pipe together, especially for MDPE and HDPE systems. (Wikipedia)
Bakit mali ang iba? Fusion weld is too generic here. Solvent weld is a different joining method typically associated with certain plastics like PVC, not this built-in-heating-element system. Caulking is sealing, not pipe fusion. So kapag may clue na built-in electric heating elements in the fittings, automatic dapat ang sagot mo: Electrofusion. (PE100+ Association)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Built-in heating element fittings for HDPE/MDPE = Electrofusion.

Bakit mali ang iba? Electrofusion ay para sa certain plastic pipe systems, hindi copper. Caulking ay old cast-iron joint method. Welding puwedeng metal-related pakinggan, pero hindi iyan ang normal ALE answer para sa copper plumbing tube. Elimination trick: kapag copper tube + plumbing, think solder, hindi weld. (Oatey)

question: Type of vent used for island sink counter?
keyMemory: Island sink counter = Loop Vent.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Loop Vent.
Ito classic plumbing venting question. Kapag island sink or freestanding sink counter ang usapan, ang ginagamit ay loop vent because there is no nearby wall where a normal vent can rise directly. Plumbing references specifically describe loop vents as the venting solution for island or free-standing sinks. (InspectApedia)
Bakit mali ang iba? Vent stack is the main vertical vent. Relief vent is for pressure relief in special venting conditions. Branch vent connects vents together to a vent stack or stack vent. Yoke vent is used in stack venting arrangements, not for island sink counters. So ang clue talaga ay iyong phrase na island sink. Kapag nakita mo iyan, halos ibinibigay na ang sagot. (InspectApedia)
Elimination method:
Island sink = walang convenient wall vent. So huwag kang pipili ng main-stack terms. Hanapin mo iyong special venting arrangement for island fixtures. Iyan si Loop Vent.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Island sink counter = Loop Vent.

question: Vent that connects the Vent Stack and Stack Vent.
keyMemory: Connecting vent(s) to the vent stack or stack vent = Branch Vent.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Branch Vent.
Ito, kailangan mo talagang pansinin ang exact wording. A branch vent is a vent pipe that connects one or more individual vents to a vent stack or stack vent. That wording matches the definition almost directly. (Studocu)
Bakit hindi yoke vent? Kasi ang yoke vent is a more specific connection involving stacks, usually between a soil/waste stack and a vent stack for pressure relief in stack systems. Pero ang tanong dito ay mas direct: connects the vent stack and stack vent. Iyan ang mas malapit sa branch vent definition in the references. Relief vent is another common trap, pero hindi iyan ang direct wording na tumutugma sa item. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Kapag ang definition ay tunog connector vent between vents and stack/stack vent, isipin mo muna ang branch vent bago ka pumunta sa mas specialized vent types.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Connecting vent(s) to the vent stack or stack vent = Branch Vent.

question: Which of the following power plants has the highest carbon emission?
keyMemory: Highest carbon emission among those choices = Fossil Fuel Power Plant.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Fossil Fuel Power Plant.
Ito environmental-energy logic na very basic. Fossil-fuel power plants produce the highest carbon emissions among those choices because they generate electricity by burning carbon-based fuels. EPA says the largest source of greenhouse gas emissions from human activities comes from burning fossil fuels, and fossil-fuel power plants emit far more CO2 than hydro, nuclear, or geothermal plants. (US EPA)
Bakit mali ang iba? Nuclear and hydro are low-carbon in operation relative to fossil fuels. Geothermal can have some emissions, but not at the same level as fossil-fuel plants. So kahit hindi mo kabisado ang exact emission values, ang elimination mo dapat ay: remove the non-combustion or lower-carbon plants first, then the obvious high-carbon answer left is fossil fuel. (US EPA)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Highest carbon emission among those choices = Fossil Fuel Power Plant.

question: Which of the following is the BEST description of a CRI?

choices.text: Ability of a lamp to either enhance or ruin the true color of an object

answer: C. Ability of a lamp to either enhance or ruin the true color of an object
keyMemory: CRI = gaano katotoo ang kulay ng bagay sa ilalim ng ilaw.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay ability of a lamp to either enhance or ruin the true color of an object.
Ang CRI or Color Rendering Index is about how faithfully a light source reveals the colors of objects compared with a reference light source. So among the choices, iyong may idea ng true color appearance of an object under a lamp ang tama. (Wikipedia)
Bakit mali ang iba? Life expectancy, power consumption, at unit intensity are all different lamp characteristics. Color of an object is not CRI either, because CRI is not the object's color itself; it is the lamp's ability to render that color properly. Kaya ang pinaka-load-bearing keyword dito ay "true color." Iyan ang pinakamalakas na clue. (NVC Lighting)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
CRI = gaano katotoo ang kulay ng bagay sa ilalim ng ilaw.

question: Who is the Professional responsible for signing and sealing Fire Sprinkler System for Building Permit?
keyMemory: Sa building-permit context, fire sprinkler / fire protection plan = PME ang sign-and-seal professional.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Professional Mechanical Engineer.
Sa Philippine building-permit practice, mechanical plans, including fire protection/fire suppression plans where applicable, are signed and sealed by a Professional Mechanical Engineer. Official LGU building-permit checklists and the NBC framework point mechanical plans to the mechanical engineer. Taguig's building office checklist, for example, explicitly lists Mechanical Plans / Fire Protection Plan as signed and sealed by a duly licensed Professional Mechanical Engineer. (Taguig City)
Bakit mali ang iba? Professional Electrical Engineer is for electrical plans. Master Plumber and Sanitary Engineer handle plumbing/sanitary scope, not sprinkler fire-protection plans in this permit context. Bureau of Fire Officer reviews and regulates under fire safety compliance, but is not the design professional who signs and seals the plan for the permit. So the ALE way to think here is: sprinkler/fire protection under MEP permit documents = Mechanical. (Pasig City Assets)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa building-permit context, fire sprinkler / fire protection plan = PME ang sign-and-seal professional.

question: Considering Code, Which of the following is an ideal location of a septic?

choices.text: 150mm away from the building perimeter wall
At the back of the house

answer: D. At the back of the house
keyMemory: Kapag septic-tank location item ay flawed, alisin mo ang under the building at sobrang lapit sa wall. Dito, back of the house ang pinakamalakas na natitira.
explanation:
Ulit: sa choices na ibinigay, ang best answer ay At the back of the house.
Magiging maingat tayo rito. Official Philippine sanitation guidance says septic tanks shall not be installed under the building unless special provisions are made. So choices under the house and under the toilet are already weak or outright undesirable as general answers. Also, 150 mm from the building perimeter wall is obviously too close to be a sensible code-based or sanitary planning answer. Among the listed options, the most defensible ideal location is at the back of the house. (weap-phil.org)
Ito ang tamang board logic: kapag hindi perfect ang wording ng item, alisin mo muna ang obviously wrong choices. Septic tanks should not just be tucked under the building, and they need practical clearance and access. Kaya kahit hindi sinasabi ng code na "back of the house" as a universal phrase, iyon ang best answer among the choices given. Iyan ang ALE elimination skill na gusto mong sanayin. (weap-phil.org)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag septic-tank location item ay flawed, alisin mo ang under the building at sobrang lapit sa wall. Dito, back of the house ang pinakamalakas na natitira.

question: Which of the following lamp is ideal for long tunnels?
keyMemory: Sa old-school lighting reviewer logic, long tunnels = LPS.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay LPS.
For classic tunnel-lighting applications, LPS or Low Pressure Sodium was used for many years because of its very high efficiency and long life, especially in long tunnel environments. Tunnel-lighting references describe LPS as a lamp type used for long tunnels and note that it was used for ages because of efficiency and lifetime. (thornlighting.com)
Bakit mali ang iba? Mercury vapor and metal halide can be used in certain lighting contexts, but in this classic reviewer question, the expected tunnel answer is LPS. CFL is clearly not the strongest choice for long-tunnel duty. Ang trap dito ay metal halide because of better color appearance, but the reviewer item is keyed to the traditional tunnel-lighting answer: LPS. (thornlighting.com)

question: Which of the following has a filament
keyMemory: Incandescent and Halogen both have filaments. Metal halide does not.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay A and B.
Incandescent lamps have a filament, and halogen lamps are actually a type of incandescent lamp that also uses a tungsten filament. DOE and Britannica both describe incandescent lamps as filament lamps and halogen lamps as incandescent lamps with a tungsten filament in a halogen-filled envelope. (The Department of Energy's Energy.gov)
Bakit mali ang iba? Metal halide is a discharge lamp, not a filament lamp in the same sense. So A and C is wrong. The examiner is testing whether you know that halogen is still a filament lamp, even if it is a more advanced form of incandescent. Kaya ang natitira is A and B. (Canon Global)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Incandescent and Halogen both have filaments.
Metal halide does not.
Sige, tuloy tayo.

question: Which of the following can be used as "high bay lamp"?
answer: D. All of the given choices
keyMemory: Kapag lahat ng listed lamp types ay valid for the application, sagot mo dapat ay All of the given choices.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay All of the given choices.
Ito, magandang halimbawa ito ng "all of the above" trap na dapat hindi ka natatakot sagutin kapag tama naman talaga lahat. Sa high-bay applications, especially sa classic industrial and warehouse lighting, commonly used HID lamp types include high-pressure sodium, metal halide, at mercury vapor. High-bay lighting references explicitly mention those three as conventional high-bay lamp options. (stouchlighting.com)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Dahil lahat ng nasa choices na lamp types ay puwedeng gamitin sa high-bay settings, lalo na sa older or conventional installations.
Bakit hindi lang isa?
Kasi ang tanong ay "which of the following can be used", hindi "which is the best". Ibig sabihin, permissive ang wording. Kapag lahat ay valid, doon ka sa all of the given choices.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices as final answer?
Hindi sila "mali" individually. Pero incomplete sila.
Kapag may all-of-the-above at lahat ng individual choices ay tama, iyon ang mas kumpletong sagot.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Valid ba ang HPS for high bay? Yes.
• Valid ba ang metal halide? Yes.
• Valid ba ang mercury vapor? Yes.
• So huwag matakot sa all of the given choices.
Common trap sa board exam
Maraming examinee ang umiiwas sa "all of the above" dahil iniisip nila panggulo lang iyon.
Mali.
Kapag na-verify mong tama lahat ng nasa a, b, c, then d is the smartest answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag lahat ng listed lamp types ay valid for the application, sagot mo dapat ay All of the given choices.

question: Identify?
keyMemory: Kapag roof-mounted tubular daylighting device ang image, sagot ay Solatube.
explanation:
Ulit: base sa image, ang best answer ay Solatube.
Magiging honest ako rito: image-based ito at medyo grainy ang print. Pero base sa itsura ng roof-mounted tube assembly with flashing, pinaka-malakas ang Solatube. Solatube systems are tubular skylights / tubular daylighting devices that use a roof-mounted collector and reflective tube to bring daylight indoors. (Solatube)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Yung image looks like a roof-mounted tubular daylighting device, not a plain vent pipe and not a lamp.
Ang shape niya ay:
• roof flashing base
• vertical tube body
• top daylight-collector/dome-like head
Iyan ang mas tugma sa Solatube kaysa sa simple vent pipe.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Vent Pipe — too plain; usually wala iyang ganitong daylighting assembly form.
High Hat Lamp — ceiling luminaire iyon, hindi roof penetration device.
Exhaust Vent — possible sana kung hooded vent lang, pero mas mukhang daylight tube kaysa exhaust terminal.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Lamp ba ito? Hindi.
• Ordinary vent pipe ba ito? Mukhang hindi.
• Roof penetration na may tube system ba ito? Oo.
• Then Solatube ang strongest answer.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay vent pipe, kasi parehong dumadaan sa roof.
Pero tingnan mo ang purpose-looking form:
vent pipe is simpler; this one looks like a daylighting device.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag roof-mounted tubular daylighting device ang image, sagot ay Solatube.

question: How to connect hub and spigot cast iron ?

Classic plumbing terminology ito. Traditional hub-and-spigot cast iron joints are made by caulking. CISPI and cast-iron installation references describe the caulked joint as the classic method for hub-and-spigot cast iron soil pipe. (Cast Iron Soil Pipe Institute)

Kapag hub and spigot cast iron ang clue, i-associate mo agad sa caulked joint.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay compression fittings, kasi sa modern practice may mechanical alternatives.
Pero kapag classic reviewer phrasing ang ginamit, caulking ang expected answer.

The wording itself gives you the clue:
• hubless pipes
• therefore you expect a hubless/no-hub coupling

Caulking — for hub-and-spigot.

question:
Identify?
(tool shown)
keyMemory: No-hub coupling tightening tool = Torque Wrench ang best formal answer dito.
explanation:
Ulit: base sa image, ang best answer ay Torque Wrench.
Magiging maingat ako rito. The image looks like a T-handle no-hub torque wrench — the specialized tool used for tightening hubless cast-iron couplings to the proper torque. Product references for no-hub cast-iron couplings describe these as preset T-handle torque wrenches. (Mainline Collection)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kasi while plumbers may casually call it a no-hub or hubless wrench, the more exact tool type is torque wrench.
Bakit hindi Hubless Wrench?
Close siya. In fact, puwede siyang tawaging no-hub wrench in the field. Pero sa choices, Torque Wrench is the better formal identification of the tool type.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Allen Wrench — hex key iyon, ibang form.
Plumber's Tee — not a wrench at all.
Hubless Wrench — close, but less formal than torque wrench.
Elimination method
Kapag image ay:
• T-handle
• socket end
• used on no-hub couplings
isipin mo: specialized torque wrench.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay Hubless Wrench, kasi field name iyon.
Pero kapag may mas exact engineering/tool term, iyon ang pipiliin mo.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
No-hub coupling tightening tool = Torque Wrench ang best formal answer dito.

the item is asking the sprinkler orientation/type, not just where the pipe is.

question: Which of the following valve has a capacity to control or regulate the flow of water?
keyMemory: Flow regulation = Globe Valve.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Globe Valve.
Ito classic valve-function question.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang globe valve ang best known sa throttling or flow regulation.
Gate valves are best for full open/full close.
Ball valves too are better for quick shutoff than fine regulation.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Gate Valve — not ideal for regulating flow; isolation valve more than control valve.
Curb Valve — service-side shutoff function, not the classic regulating answer.
Ball Valve — on/off oriented; not the preferred fine-control valve in reviewer logic.
Elimination method
Tandaan mo ito:
• Gate = isolate
• Ball = quick shutoff
• Globe = regulate
Kapag may word na control or regulate, globe agad ang isipin mo.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay ball valve, dahil puwede mo rin naman i-half-open.
Oo, pero hindi iyon ang best-regulating valve answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Flow regulation = Globe Valve.

Bakit ito ang tama sa reviewer logic?

• Between 0.5% and none, reviewer logic prefers 0.5%.

Ang trap dito ay none, especially if iniisip mo pressure line naman ito.
Pero sa reviewer setting, they often expect a small slope for drainage/maintenance practicality.

question: What is the most economical conduit for communication line?
keyMemory: Communication line + pinaka-matipid = PVC conduit.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay PVC.
Ang clue dito ay "most economical" at communication line. For low-voltage/communications use, PVC conduit is widely used because it is lightweight and affordable, and PVC conduit is commonly promoted as one of the most economical options for low-voltage runs. (Shielden)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kung communication line lang ang usapan, hindi mo kailangan agad ng mas mabigat at mas mahal na metal conduit kung walang special condition. Kaya sa board logic, ang PVC ang pinaka-practical at pinaka-matipid.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
EMT, IMC, at RSC are all metallic options, and generally mas mahal sila kaysa PVC. IMC at RSC are heavier-duty pa lalo, kaya lalo silang hindi "most economical." (Shielden)
Elimination method
Kapag nakita mo ang phrase na most economical for communications, alisin mo muna ang mas heavy-duty metal conduits. Matitira si PVC.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Communication line + pinaka-matipid = PVC conduit.

Ulit: ang tamang sagot dito ay Koppel.
Ito, ang outlier logic ang gamitin mo. Koppel is clearly marketed as an air-conditioner brand in the Philippines, while Himel, Schneider, Omni, Koten, at Royu/Roya are sold as breaker/electrical brands. (Himel)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa choices, si Koppel ang pinaka-obvious na hindi breaker brand ang reviewer intent. Aircon brand siya.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Hindi ko sasabihing perfect ang item, pero enough na makita mong may isang malinaw na aircon brand sa gitna ng breaker brands.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Hanapin ang outlier
• Hindi technical specs ang tanong, brand-family recognition ang tanong
• If one brand is strongly associated with air-conditioning, iyon ang "except"
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay baka malito ka sa Roya dahil likely typo siya for Royu. Pero kahit may typo, si Koppel pa rin ang pinakamalinaw na outlier.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may isang aircon brand sa listahan ng breaker brands, iyon ang "except." Dito, Koppel.

question: All are aircon brands except?

Hanabishi sells air conditioners, American Home sells air conditioners, and even Asahi currently markets portable aircon products. So based on currently available market evidence, walang clean "except." (Hanabishi)

Elimination method

• Kapag item is flawed, aminin mo
• Then pick the most likely intended answer
• Huwag magpanggap na perfect ang question kung hindi naman

This item is ambiguous.

Kapag dalawang activities ang binigay at magkaiba ang ideal color temp nila, hanapin ang middle-ground answer.

question: Which of the following is an ideal fire sprinkler system for open parking building?

explanation:
Ulit: sa reviewer logic, ang best answer ay Dry Type.

Open parking structures are often discussed as a special case, and some code discussions even note that open parking structures may be unsprinklered depending on the situation. But if sprinkler protection is provided, reviewer logic commonly points to dry-type systems for open or exposed parking structures. Industry discussions on open parking structures frequently reference dry-pipe systems for these applications. (National Fire Sprinkler Association)

Because the setting is open parking building, not a normal conditioned interior space.
Among the four types, dry type is the classic special-condition answer.

Wet type is the default for heated/interior spaces.
Pre-action is for accidental-discharge-sensitive spaces.
Deluge is for special high-hazard applications.
Elimination method
Kapag space is more exposed/open and not a typical conditioned occupancy, reviewer logic pushes you away from wet type and toward dry type.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Open parking sprinkler question = Dry Type ang usual reviewer answer.

question: It is a unit made of several devices, which uses visual and audio signalization to warn people about possible fire or smoke occurrence around coverage area.
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay warning by sound and light, ang sagot ay Fire Alarm, hindi smoke detector.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Fire Alarm.
Dito, ang pinakaimportanteng clue ay "uses visual and audio signalization to warn people."
Ibig sabihin, hindi lang ito sensor. Hindi lang ito simpleng detector. Isa itong warning system.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang fire alarm system ang may kakayahang magbigay ng:
• audio signal tulad ng bell, horn, buzzer
• visual signal tulad ng strobe or warning light
Ang smoke detector kasi puwedeng part lang ng system. Pero ang tanong ay mas malawak: unit made of several devices na nagwa-warning sa tao. Iyan ang mas tumutukoy sa fire alarm.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Smoke Detector — detector lang ito. Sensing device siya, hindi ang buong warning unit/system.
c. Fire Signal — masyadong generic at hindi standard term ng system.
d. Fire Protection — sobrang broad. Kasama rito sprinklers, extinguishers, alarms, exits, at iba pa.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
• Sensor lang ba ang tinutukoy?
• O buong warning setup?
Kapag may audio + visual warning, huwag kang tumigil sa detector.
Hanapin mo ang system term, which is Fire Alarm.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay Smoke Detector, dahil may smoke occurrence sa tanong.
Pero tandaan:
smoke detector detects
fire alarm warns
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay warning by sound and light, ang sagot ay Fire Alarm, hindi smoke detector.

• alin ang may pinaka-maraming accessory requirement?
• alin ang may mas komplikadong installation?
Diyan lamang si wall-hung ang malakas.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay flushometer type, kasi commercial at mukhang mas mahal.
Pero tandaan:
wall-hung usually has hidden carrier and structural support issues, kaya mas mahal ang full setup.

question: Ideal application of acoustic panel for narrow spaces?
keyMemory: Sa narrow spaces, wall acoustic panels ang pinaka-practical na unang sagot.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Wall.
Dito, spatial reasoning ang clue. Kapag narrow spaces, ayaw mong lalo pang kumain ng headroom or circulation unnecessarily.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa narrow spaces, wall-mounted acoustic treatment is often the most practical because:
• hindi nito pinapakialaman ang floor use
• hindi rin agad kumakain ng ceiling depth if limited na ang vertical clearance
• mas direct pa ang treatment sa lateral reflections
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Floor — hindi ito practical as acoustic panel application in narrow space.
b. Ceiling — possible, pero kung narrow ang concern, mas practical muna ang wall treatment.
d. Furniture — not the primary application asked here.
Elimination method
Ganito ang isip:
• narrow ba ang issue? yes
• saan pinakamadaling i-apply without blocking floor use? wall
• floor and furniture are weak answers
• ceiling is possible, but wall is the stronger reviewer answer
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay ceiling, kasi acoustic treatment often goes there.
Pero kung sinabi ng item na narrow spaces, wall application is more direct and practical.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa narrow spaces, wall acoustic panels ang pinaka-practical na unang sagot.

A foot valve is commonly installed at the end of a suction pipe, especially in pumping systems, to maintain priming and prevent backflow.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Check Valve — one-way valve siya, but not specifically "usually installed at the end of a pipe."
Gate Valve — line isolation valve ito.
Back Flow Valve — broader backflow-prevention idea, not the classic end-of-pipe answer.
Elimination method
Kapag ang clue ay:
• end of pipe
• typical pump/suction-side association
think foot valve.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay check valve, kasi one-way din naman ang foot valve.
Pero tandaan:
foot valve is basically the end-of-pipe check valve in pump suction context.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
End of suction pipe = Foot Valve.

question: It is an electrical device used to provide overcurrent and short-circuit protection for electric circuits from 800 Amps to 10k Amps.
keyMemory: High-current main protection device = ACB. Sige, last page tayo. Tuloy ang numbering.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay ACB.
Bakit ito ang tama?
ACB or Air Circuit Breaker is used for high-current applications and provides overcurrent and short-circuit protection in large systems.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
GFCI — ground-fault protection for smaller circuits/wet areas.
RCD — residual current device, leakage/ground fault protection, not this high-amp main protection context.
ATS — automatic transfer switch, not a breaker.
MTS — manual transfer switch, not a breaker.
Elimination method
Kapag ang clue ay:
• 800 A to 10k A
• overcurrent and short-circuit protection
• large electrical system
hanapin mo ang heavy-duty breaker, not fault-sensing accessory or transfer switch.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay ATS, kasi malaki rin at nasa main power setup.
Pero tandaan:
ATS transfers source
ACB breaks and protects circuit
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
High-current main protection device = ACB.
Sige, last page tayo. Tuloy ang numbering.

question: Which is not an ideal material for overhead water tank?
keyMemory: Sa overhead tanks, least ideal sa choices = concrete, mainly because of weight.
explanation:

Several tank-material discussions highlight concrete's weight and the larger support/foundation requirements it brings. (tankvision.com.au)
Bakit ito ang tama sa choices?
Hindi ibig sabihin bawal ang concrete. Ang tanong ay not ideal. Sa overhead setup, ayaw mo ng sobrang bigat kung may mas magaan at more practical materials available.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Stainless steel, polyethylene, at fiberglass are all much more reasonable overhead-tank choices because of lower weight and easier installation. (sunrisetank.com)
Elimination method
Kapag overhead ang clue, isipin mo agad ang bigat. Ang pinakamabigat ang unang tatanggalin. Dito iyon ay concrete.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa overhead tanks, least ideal sa choices = concrete, mainly because of weight.

question: Ideal mounting slope of a solar panel?
keyMemory: Sa Philippine reviewer logic, ideal solar tilt = around 20° kung one answer lang ang pipiliin.
explanation:
Ulit: ang best answer dito ay 20 degrees.
Sa Pilipinas, practical guidance says solar-panel tilt is commonly in the 10° to 30° range, depending on season and location. Metro Manila is around 14°–15°N, and south-facing panels are generally favored most of the year, so 20° is the best all-around compromise among the choices. (Solar Panels Philippines | Solaric)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kung 10° masyadong flat minsan, at kung 30° pataas medyo steeper than needed for a general-answer Philippine setting. So sa choices, 20° ang pinaka-balanced reviewer answer.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
10° can work, yes, pero usually too low as a generic "ideal" answer.
30°, 45°, 60° are increasingly steeper and less likely as the best one-size-fits-all answer here. (Solar Panels Philippines | Solaric)
Elimination method
Kapag latitude natin is low tropical latitude, huwag ka agad sa sobrang steep. Hanapin mo ang moderate angle. Dito, 20°.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa Philippine reviewer logic, ideal solar tilt = around 20° kung one answer lang ang pipiliin.

question: Best orientation of a solar panel?
keyMemory: Philippines + solar orientation = South.
explanation:
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay South.
Because the Philippines is in the Northern Hemisphere, solar panels are generally oriented south for the best yearly exposure. Philippine solar-tilt discussions specifically note that in Metro Manila the sun is on the south side most of the year, so south-facing panels maximize exposure. (Schadow1 Expeditions)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ito ang pinakaclassic north-hemisphere solar rule: face south.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
East and West favor one side of the day only.
North is the opposite of the usual annual optimum in the Northern Hemisphere.
Northeast is not the standard ideal orientation. (Schadow1 Expeditions)
Elimination method
Kapag solar orientation ang tanong, isipin mo muna kung nasa north o south hemisphere tayo. Dahil nasa Northern Hemisphere ang Philippines, sagot ay South.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Philippines + solar orientation = South.

question: Worst location/orientation of an ACCU?

question: Which of the following appliances consumes the most electricity?

Heating water is a major electrical load.

Kapag may choice na water heater, maging alerto ka agad. Heating appliances usually dominate electrical consumption.
